built with pdoc

aptos_sdk

Aptos Python SDK

A Python library for interacting with the Aptos blockchain.

Quick Start:

import asyncio from aptos_sdk import Account, RestClient, FaucetClient

async def main(): ... async with RestClient("https://fullnode.testnet.aptoslabs.com/v1") as client: ... account = Account.generate() ... faucet = FaucetClient("https://faucet.testnet.aptoslabs.com", client) ... await faucet.fund_account(account.address(), 100_000_000) ... balance = await client.account_balance(account.address()) ... print(f"Balance: {balance}")

asyncio.run(main())

For more examples, see the examples/ directory.

  1# Copyright © Aptos Foundation
  2# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
  3
  4"""
  5Aptos Python SDK
  6
  7A Python library for interacting with the Aptos blockchain.
  8
  9Quick Start:
 10    >>> import asyncio
 11    >>> from aptos_sdk import Account, RestClient, FaucetClient
 12    >>>
 13    >>> async def main():
 14    ...     async with RestClient("https://fullnode.testnet.aptoslabs.com/v1") as client:
 15    ...         account = Account.generate()
 16    ...         faucet = FaucetClient("https://faucet.testnet.aptoslabs.com", client)
 17    ...         await faucet.fund_account(account.address(), 100_000_000)
 18    ...         balance = await client.account_balance(account.address())
 19    ...         print(f"Balance: {balance}")
 20    >>>
 21    >>> asyncio.run(main())
 22
 23For more examples, see the examples/ directory.
 24"""
 25
 26__version__ = "0.12.0"
 27

__version__ = '0.12.0'
class AccountAddress: 
 31class AccountAddress:
 32    address: bytes
 33    LENGTH: int = 32
 34
 35    def __init__(self, address: bytes):
 36        self.address = address
 37
 38        if len(address) != AccountAddress.LENGTH:
 39            raise ParseAddressError("Expected address of length 32")
 40
 41    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
 42        if not isinstance(other, AccountAddress):
 43            return NotImplemented
 44        return self.address == other.address
 45
 46    def __hash__(self) -> int:
 47        return hash(self.address)
 48
 49    def __str__(self) -> str:
 50        """
 51        Represent an account address in a way that is compliant with the v1 address
 52        standard. The standard is defined as part of AIP-40, read more here:
 53        https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md
 54
 55        In short, all special addresses SHOULD be represented in SHORT form, e.g.
 56
 57        0x1
 58
 59        All other addresses MUST be represented in LONG form, e.g.
 60
 61        0x002098630cfad4734812fa37dc18d9b8d59242feabe49259e26318d468a99584
 62
 63        For an explanation of what defines a "special" address, see `is_special`.
 64
 65        All string representations of addresses MUST be prefixed with 0x.
 66        """
 67        suffix = self.address.hex()
 68        if self.is_special():
 69            suffix = suffix.lstrip("0") or "0"
 70        return f"0x{suffix}"
 71
 72    def __repr__(self) -> str:
 73        return self.__str__()
 74
 75    def is_special(self) -> bool:
 76        """
 77        Returns whether the address is a "special" address. Addresses are considered
 78        special if the first 63 characters of the hex string are zero. In other words,
 79        an address is special if the first 31 bytes are zero and the last byte is
 80        smaller than `0b10000` (16). In other words, special is defined as an address
 81        that matches the following regex: `^0x0{63}[0-9a-f]$`. In short form this means
 82        the addresses in the range from `0x0` to `0xf` (inclusive) are special.
 83
 84        For more details see the v1 address standard defined as part of AIP-40:
 85        https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md
 86        """
 87        return all(b == 0 for b in self.address[:-1]) and self.address[-1] < 0b10000
 88
 89    @staticmethod
 90    def from_str(address: str) -> AccountAddress:
 91        """
 92        NOTE: This function has strict parsing behavior. For relaxed behavior, please use
 93        `from_string_relaxed` function.
 94
 95        Creates an instance of AccountAddress from a hex string.
 96
 97        This function allows only the strictest formats defined by AIP-40. In short this
 98        means only the following formats are accepted:
 99        - LONG
100        - SHORT for special addresses
101
102        Where:
103        - LONG is defined as 0x + 64 hex characters.
104        - SHORT for special addresses is 0x0 to 0xf inclusive without padding zeroes.
105
106        This means the following are not accepted:
107        - SHORT for non-special addresses.
108        - Any address without a leading 0x.
109
110        Learn more about the different address formats by reading AIP-40:
111        https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md.
112
113        Parameters:
114        - address (str): A hex string representing an account address.
115
116        Returns:
117        - AccountAddress: An instance of AccountAddress.
118        """
119        # Assert the string starts with 0x.
120        if not address.startswith("0x"):
121            raise RuntimeError("Hex string must start with a leading 0x.")
122
123        out = AccountAddress.from_str_relaxed(address)
124
125        # Check if the address is in LONG form. If it is not, this is only allowed for
126        # special addresses, in which case we check it is in proper SHORT form.
127        if len(address) != AccountAddress.LENGTH * 2 + 2:
128            if not out.is_special():
129                raise RuntimeError(
130                    "The given hex string is not a special address, it must be represented "
131                    "as 0x + 64 chars."
132                )
133            else:
134                # 0x + one hex char is the only valid SHORT form for special addresses.
135                if len(address) != 3:
136                    raise RuntimeError(
137                        "The given hex string is a special address not in LONG form, "
138                        "it must be 0x0 to 0xf without padding zeroes."
139                    )
140
141        # Assert that only special addresses can use short form.
142        if len(address[2:]) != AccountAddress.LENGTH * 2 and not out.is_special():
143            raise RuntimeError(
144                "Padding zeroes are not allowed, the address must be represented as "
145                "0x0 to 0xf for special addresses or 0x + 64 chars for all other addresses."
146            )
147
148        return out
149
150    @staticmethod
151    def from_str_relaxed(address: str) -> AccountAddress:
152        """
153        NOTE: This function has relaxed parsing behavior. For strict behavior, please use
154        the `from_string` function. Where possible, use `from_string` rather than this
155        function. `from_string_relaxed` is only provided for backwards compatibility.
156
157        Creates an instance of AccountAddress from a hex string.
158
159        This function allows all formats defined by AIP-40. In short, this means the
160        following formats are accepted:
161        - LONG, with or without leading 0x
162        - SHORT, with or without leading 0x
163
164        Where:
165        - LONG is 64 hex characters.
166        - SHORT is 1 to 63 hex characters inclusive.
167        - Padding zeroes are allowed, e.g., 0x0123 is valid.
168
169        Learn more about the different address formats by reading AIP-40:
170        https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md.
171
172        Parameters:
173        - address (str): A hex string representing an account address.
174
175        Returns:
176        - AccountAddress: An instance of AccountAddress.
177        """
178        addr = address
179
180        # Strip 0x prefix if present.
181        if address[0:2] == "0x":
182            addr = address[2:]
183
184        # Assert the address is at least one hex char long.
185        if len(addr) < 1:
186            raise RuntimeError(
187                "Hex string is too short, must be 1 to 64 chars long, excluding the "
188                "leading 0x."
189            )
190
191        # Assert the address is at most 64 hex chars long.
192        if len(addr) > 64:
193            raise RuntimeError(
194                "Hex string is too long, must be 1 to 64 chars long, excluding the "
195                "leading 0x."
196            )
197
198        if len(addr) < AccountAddress.LENGTH * 2:
199            pad = "0" * (AccountAddress.LENGTH * 2 - len(addr))
200            addr = pad + addr
201
202        return AccountAddress(bytes.fromhex(addr))
203
204    @staticmethod
205    def from_key(key: asymmetric_crypto.PublicKey) -> AccountAddress:
206        hasher = hashlib.sha3_256()
207        hasher.update(key.to_crypto_bytes())
208
209        if isinstance(key, ed25519.PublicKey):
210            hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.Ed25519)
211        elif isinstance(key, ed25519.MultiPublicKey):
212            hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.MultiEd25519)
213        elif isinstance(key, asymmetric_crypto_wrapper.PublicKey):
214            hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.SingleKey)
215        elif isinstance(key, asymmetric_crypto_wrapper.MultiPublicKey):
216            hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.MultiKey)
217        else:
218            raise InvalidPublicKey(f"Unsupported key type: {type(key).__name__}")
219
220        return AccountAddress(hasher.digest())
221
222    @staticmethod
223    def for_resource_account(creator: AccountAddress, seed: bytes) -> AccountAddress:
224        hasher = hashlib.sha3_256()
225        hasher.update(creator.address)
226        hasher.update(seed)
227        hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.DeriveResourceAccountAddress)
228        return AccountAddress(hasher.digest())
229
230    @staticmethod
231    def for_guid_object(creator: AccountAddress, creation_num: int) -> AccountAddress:
232        hasher = hashlib.sha3_256()
233        serializer = Serializer()
234        serializer.u64(creation_num)
235        hasher.update(serializer.output())
236        hasher.update(creator.address)
237        hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.DeriveObjectAddressFromGuid)
238        return AccountAddress(hasher.digest())
239
240    @staticmethod
241    def for_named_object(creator: AccountAddress, seed: bytes) -> AccountAddress:
242        hasher = hashlib.sha3_256()
243        hasher.update(creator.address)
244        hasher.update(seed)
245        hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.DeriveObjectAddressFromSeed)
246        return AccountAddress(hasher.digest())
247
248    @staticmethod
249    def for_named_token(
250        creator: AccountAddress, collection_name: str, token_name: str
251    ) -> AccountAddress:
252        collection_bytes = collection_name.encode()
253        token_bytes = token_name.encode()
254        return AccountAddress.for_named_object(
255            creator, collection_bytes + b"::" + token_bytes
256        )
257
258    @staticmethod
259    def for_named_collection(
260        creator: AccountAddress, collection_name: str
261    ) -> AccountAddress:
262        return AccountAddress.for_named_object(creator, collection_name.encode())
263
264    @staticmethod
265    def deserialize(deserializer: Deserializer) -> AccountAddress:
266        return AccountAddress(deserializer.fixed_bytes(AccountAddress.LENGTH))
267
268    def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None:
269        serializer.fixed_bytes(self.address)
AccountAddress(address: bytes) 
35    def __init__(self, address: bytes):
36        self.address = address
37
38        if len(address) != AccountAddress.LENGTH:
39            raise ParseAddressError("Expected address of length 32")
address: bytes
LENGTH: int = 32
def is_special(self) -> bool: 
75    def is_special(self) -> bool:
76        """
77        Returns whether the address is a "special" address. Addresses are considered
78        special if the first 63 characters of the hex string are zero. In other words,
79        an address is special if the first 31 bytes are zero and the last byte is
80        smaller than `0b10000` (16). In other words, special is defined as an address
81        that matches the following regex: `^0x0{63}[0-9a-f]$`. In short form this means
82        the addresses in the range from `0x0` to `0xf` (inclusive) are special.
83
84        For more details see the v1 address standard defined as part of AIP-40:
85        https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md
86        """
87        return all(b == 0 for b in self.address[:-1]) and self.address[-1] < 0b10000

Returns whether the address is a "special" address. Addresses are considered special if the first 63 characters of the hex string are zero. In other words, an address is special if the first 31 bytes are zero and the last byte is smaller than 0b10000 (16). In other words, special is defined as an address that matches the following regex: ^0x0{63}[0-9a-f]$. In short form this means the addresses in the range from 0x0 to 0xf (inclusive) are special.

For more details see the v1 address standard defined as part of AIP-40: https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md

@staticmethod
def from_str(address: str) -> AccountAddress: 
 89    @staticmethod
 90    def from_str(address: str) -> AccountAddress:
 91        """
 92        NOTE: This function has strict parsing behavior. For relaxed behavior, please use
 93        `from_string_relaxed` function.
 94
 95        Creates an instance of AccountAddress from a hex string.
 96
 97        This function allows only the strictest formats defined by AIP-40. In short this
 98        means only the following formats are accepted:
 99        - LONG
100        - SHORT for special addresses
101
102        Where:
103        - LONG is defined as 0x + 64 hex characters.
104        - SHORT for special addresses is 0x0 to 0xf inclusive without padding zeroes.
105
106        This means the following are not accepted:
107        - SHORT for non-special addresses.
108        - Any address without a leading 0x.
109
110        Learn more about the different address formats by reading AIP-40:
111        https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md.
112
113        Parameters:
114        - address (str): A hex string representing an account address.
115
116        Returns:
117        - AccountAddress: An instance of AccountAddress.
118        """
119        # Assert the string starts with 0x.
120        if not address.startswith("0x"):
121            raise RuntimeError("Hex string must start with a leading 0x.")
122
123        out = AccountAddress.from_str_relaxed(address)
124
125        # Check if the address is in LONG form. If it is not, this is only allowed for
126        # special addresses, in which case we check it is in proper SHORT form.
127        if len(address) != AccountAddress.LENGTH * 2 + 2:
128            if not out.is_special():
129                raise RuntimeError(
130                    "The given hex string is not a special address, it must be represented "
131                    "as 0x + 64 chars."
132                )
133            else:
134                # 0x + one hex char is the only valid SHORT form for special addresses.
135                if len(address) != 3:
136                    raise RuntimeError(
137                        "The given hex string is a special address not in LONG form, "
138                        "it must be 0x0 to 0xf without padding zeroes."
139                    )
140
141        # Assert that only special addresses can use short form.
142        if len(address[2:]) != AccountAddress.LENGTH * 2 and not out.is_special():
143            raise RuntimeError(
144                "Padding zeroes are not allowed, the address must be represented as "
145                "0x0 to 0xf for special addresses or 0x + 64 chars for all other addresses."
146            )
147
148        return out

NOTE: This function has strict parsing behavior. For relaxed behavior, please use from_string_relaxed function.

Creates an instance of AccountAddress from a hex string.

This function allows only the strictest formats defined by AIP-40. In short this means only the following formats are accepted:

  • LONG
  • SHORT for special addresses

Where:

  • LONG is defined as 0x + 64 hex characters.
  • SHORT for special addresses is 0x0 to 0xf inclusive without padding zeroes.

This means the following are not accepted:

  • SHORT for non-special addresses.
  • Any address without a leading 0x.

Learn more about the different address formats by reading AIP-40: https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md.

Parameters:

  • address (str): A hex string representing an account address.

Returns:

  • AccountAddress: An instance of AccountAddress.
@staticmethod
def from_str_relaxed(address: str) -> AccountAddress: 
150    @staticmethod
151    def from_str_relaxed(address: str) -> AccountAddress:
152        """
153        NOTE: This function has relaxed parsing behavior. For strict behavior, please use
154        the `from_string` function. Where possible, use `from_string` rather than this
155        function. `from_string_relaxed` is only provided for backwards compatibility.
156
157        Creates an instance of AccountAddress from a hex string.
158
159        This function allows all formats defined by AIP-40. In short, this means the
160        following formats are accepted:
161        - LONG, with or without leading 0x
162        - SHORT, with or without leading 0x
163
164        Where:
165        - LONG is 64 hex characters.
166        - SHORT is 1 to 63 hex characters inclusive.
167        - Padding zeroes are allowed, e.g., 0x0123 is valid.
168
169        Learn more about the different address formats by reading AIP-40:
170        https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md.
171
172        Parameters:
173        - address (str): A hex string representing an account address.
174
175        Returns:
176        - AccountAddress: An instance of AccountAddress.
177        """
178        addr = address
179
180        # Strip 0x prefix if present.
181        if address[0:2] == "0x":
182            addr = address[2:]
183
184        # Assert the address is at least one hex char long.
185        if len(addr) < 1:
186            raise RuntimeError(
187                "Hex string is too short, must be 1 to 64 chars long, excluding the "
188                "leading 0x."
189            )
190
191        # Assert the address is at most 64 hex chars long.
192        if len(addr) > 64:
193            raise RuntimeError(
194                "Hex string is too long, must be 1 to 64 chars long, excluding the "
195                "leading 0x."
196            )
197
198        if len(addr) < AccountAddress.LENGTH * 2:
199            pad = "0" * (AccountAddress.LENGTH * 2 - len(addr))
200            addr = pad + addr
201
202        return AccountAddress(bytes.fromhex(addr))

NOTE: This function has relaxed parsing behavior. For strict behavior, please use the from_string function. Where possible, use from_string rather than this function. from_string_relaxed is only provided for backwards compatibility.

Creates an instance of AccountAddress from a hex string.

This function allows all formats defined by AIP-40. In short, this means the following formats are accepted:

  • LONG, with or without leading 0x
  • SHORT, with or without leading 0x

Where:

  • LONG is 64 hex characters.
  • SHORT is 1 to 63 hex characters inclusive.
  • Padding zeroes are allowed, e.g., 0x0123 is valid.

Learn more about the different address formats by reading AIP-40: https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-40.md.

Parameters:

  • address (str): A hex string representing an account address.

Returns:

  • AccountAddress: An instance of AccountAddress.
@staticmethod
def from_key( key: PublicKey) -> AccountAddress: 
204    @staticmethod
205    def from_key(key: asymmetric_crypto.PublicKey) -> AccountAddress:
206        hasher = hashlib.sha3_256()
207        hasher.update(key.to_crypto_bytes())
208
209        if isinstance(key, ed25519.PublicKey):
210            hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.Ed25519)
211        elif isinstance(key, ed25519.MultiPublicKey):
212            hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.MultiEd25519)
213        elif isinstance(key, asymmetric_crypto_wrapper.PublicKey):
214            hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.SingleKey)
215        elif isinstance(key, asymmetric_crypto_wrapper.MultiPublicKey):
216            hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.MultiKey)
217        else:
218            raise InvalidPublicKey(f"Unsupported key type: {type(key).__name__}")
219
220        return AccountAddress(hasher.digest())
@staticmethod
def for_resource_account( creator: AccountAddress, seed: bytes) -> AccountAddress: 
222    @staticmethod
223    def for_resource_account(creator: AccountAddress, seed: bytes) -> AccountAddress:
224        hasher = hashlib.sha3_256()
225        hasher.update(creator.address)
226        hasher.update(seed)
227        hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.DeriveResourceAccountAddress)
228        return AccountAddress(hasher.digest())
@staticmethod
def for_guid_object( creator: AccountAddress, creation_num: int) -> AccountAddress: 
230    @staticmethod
231    def for_guid_object(creator: AccountAddress, creation_num: int) -> AccountAddress:
232        hasher = hashlib.sha3_256()
233        serializer = Serializer()
234        serializer.u64(creation_num)
235        hasher.update(serializer.output())
236        hasher.update(creator.address)
237        hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.DeriveObjectAddressFromGuid)
238        return AccountAddress(hasher.digest())
@staticmethod
def for_named_object( creator: AccountAddress, seed: bytes) -> AccountAddress: 
240    @staticmethod
241    def for_named_object(creator: AccountAddress, seed: bytes) -> AccountAddress:
242        hasher = hashlib.sha3_256()
243        hasher.update(creator.address)
244        hasher.update(seed)
245        hasher.update(AuthKeyScheme.DeriveObjectAddressFromSeed)
246        return AccountAddress(hasher.digest())
@staticmethod
def for_named_token( creator: AccountAddress, collection_name: str, token_name: str) -> AccountAddress: 
248    @staticmethod
249    def for_named_token(
250        creator: AccountAddress, collection_name: str, token_name: str
251    ) -> AccountAddress:
252        collection_bytes = collection_name.encode()
253        token_bytes = token_name.encode()
254        return AccountAddress.for_named_object(
255            creator, collection_bytes + b"::" + token_bytes
256        )
@staticmethod
def for_named_collection( creator: AccountAddress, collection_name: str) -> AccountAddress: 
258    @staticmethod
259    def for_named_collection(
260        creator: AccountAddress, collection_name: str
261    ) -> AccountAddress:
262        return AccountAddress.for_named_object(creator, collection_name.encode())
@staticmethod
def deserialize( deserializer: Deserializer) -> AccountAddress: 
264    @staticmethod
265    def deserialize(deserializer: Deserializer) -> AccountAddress:
266        return AccountAddress(deserializer.fixed_bytes(AccountAddress.LENGTH))
def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None: 
268    def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None:
269        serializer.fixed_bytes(self.address)
class Deserializer: 
 76class Deserializer:
 77    """
 78    BCS deserializer for reading typed values from a byte stream.
 79
 80    Reads primitive types (integers, strings, bytes) and complex structures
 81    from BCS-encoded bytes.
 82
 83    Example:
 84        >>> der = Deserializer(b"\\x05hello")
 85        >>> text = der.str()  # "hello"
 86    """
 87
 88    _input: io.BytesIO
 89    _length: int
 90
 91    def __init__(self, data: bytes):
 92        self._length = len(data)
 93        self._input = io.BytesIO(data)
 94
 95    def remaining(self) -> int:
 96        return self._length - self._input.tell()
 97
 98    def bool(self) -> bool:
 99        value = int.from_bytes(self._read(1), byteorder="little", signed=False)
100        if value == 0:
101            return False
102        elif value == 1:
103            return True
104        else:
105            raise DeserializationError(f"Unexpected boolean value: {value}")
106
107    def to_bytes(self) -> bytes:
108        return self._read(self.uleb128())
109
110    def fixed_bytes(self, length: int) -> bytes:
111        return self._read(length)
112
113    def map(
114        self,
115        key_decoder: typing.Callable[[Deserializer], typing.Any],
116        value_decoder: typing.Callable[[Deserializer], typing.Any],
117    ) -> Dict[typing.Any, typing.Any]:
118        length = self.uleb128()
119        values: Dict = {}
120        while len(values) < length:
121            key = key_decoder(self)
122            value = value_decoder(self)
123            values[key] = value
124        return values
125
126    def sequence(
127        self,
128        value_decoder: typing.Callable[[Deserializer], typing.Any],
129    ) -> List[typing.Any]:
130        length = self.uleb128()
131        values: List = []
132        while len(values) < length:
133            values.append(value_decoder(self))
134        return values
135
136    def str(self) -> str:
137        return self.to_bytes().decode()
138
139    def struct(self, struct: typing.Any) -> typing.Any:
140        return struct.deserialize(self)
141
142    def u8(self) -> int:
143        return self._read_int(1)
144
145    def u16(self) -> int:
146        return self._read_int(2)
147
148    def u32(self) -> int:
149        return self._read_int(4)
150
151    def u64(self) -> int:
152        return self._read_int(8)
153
154    def u128(self) -> int:
155        return self._read_int(16)
156
157    def u256(self) -> int:
158        return self._read_int(32)
159
160    def uleb128(self) -> int:
161        value = 0
162        shift = 0
163
164        while value <= MAX_U32:
165            byte = self._read_int(1)
166            value |= (byte & 0x7F) << shift
167            if byte & 0x80 == 0:
168                break
169            shift += 7
170
171        if value > MAX_U32:
172            raise DeserializationError("Unexpectedly large uleb128 value")
173
174        return value
175
176    def _read(self, length: int) -> bytes:
177        value = self._input.read(length)
178        if value is None or len(value) < length:
179            actual_length = 0 if value is None else len(value)
180            raise DeserializationError(
181                f"Unexpected end of input. Requested: {length}, found: {actual_length}"
182            )
183        return value
184
185    def _read_int(self, length: int) -> int:
186        return int.from_bytes(self._read(length), byteorder="little", signed=False)

BCS deserializer for reading typed values from a byte stream.

Reads primitive types (integers, strings, bytes) and complex structures from BCS-encoded bytes.

Example:

der = Deserializer(b"\x05hello") text = der.str() # "hello"

Deserializer(data: bytes) 
91    def __init__(self, data: bytes):
92        self._length = len(data)
93        self._input = io.BytesIO(data)
def remaining(self) -> int: 
95    def remaining(self) -> int:
96        return self._length - self._input.tell()
def bool(self) -> <function Deserializer.bool at 0x10ac9e980>: 
 98    def bool(self) -> bool:
 99        value = int.from_bytes(self._read(1), byteorder="little", signed=False)
100        if value == 0:
101            return False
102        elif value == 1:
103            return True
104        else:
105            raise DeserializationError(f"Unexpected boolean value: {value}")
def to_bytes(self) -> bytes: 
107    def to_bytes(self) -> bytes:
108        return self._read(self.uleb128())
def fixed_bytes(self, length: int) -> bytes: 
110    def fixed_bytes(self, length: int) -> bytes:
111        return self._read(length)
def map( self, key_decoder: Callable[[Deserializer], Any], value_decoder: Callable[[Deserializer], Any]) -> Dict[Any, Any]: 
113    def map(
114        self,
115        key_decoder: typing.Callable[[Deserializer], typing.Any],
116        value_decoder: typing.Callable[[Deserializer], typing.Any],
117    ) -> Dict[typing.Any, typing.Any]:
118        length = self.uleb128()
119        values: Dict = {}
120        while len(values) < length:
121            key = key_decoder(self)
122            value = value_decoder(self)
123            values[key] = value
124        return values
def sequence( self, value_decoder: Callable[[Deserializer], Any]) -> List[Any]: 
126    def sequence(
127        self,
128        value_decoder: typing.Callable[[Deserializer], typing.Any],
129    ) -> List[typing.Any]:
130        length = self.uleb128()
131        values: List = []
132        while len(values) < length:
133            values.append(value_decoder(self))
134        return values
def str(self) -> <function Deserializer.str at 0x10ac9eca0>: 
136    def str(self) -> str:
137        return self.to_bytes().decode()
def struct(self, struct: Any) -> Any: 
139    def struct(self, struct: typing.Any) -> typing.Any:
140        return struct.deserialize(self)
def u8(self) -> int: 
142    def u8(self) -> int:
143        return self._read_int(1)
def u16(self) -> int: 
145    def u16(self) -> int:
146        return self._read_int(2)
def u32(self) -> int: 
148    def u32(self) -> int:
149        return self._read_int(4)
def u64(self) -> int: 
151    def u64(self) -> int:
152        return self._read_int(8)
def u128(self) -> int: 
154    def u128(self) -> int:
155        return self._read_int(16)
def u256(self) -> int: 
157    def u256(self) -> int:
158        return self._read_int(32)
def uleb128(self) -> int: 
160    def uleb128(self) -> int:
161        value = 0
162        shift = 0
163
164        while value <= MAX_U32:
165            byte = self._read_int(1)
166            value |= (byte & 0x7F) << shift
167            if byte & 0x80 == 0:
168                break
169            shift += 7
170
171        if value > MAX_U32:
172            raise DeserializationError("Unexpectedly large uleb128 value")
173
174        return value
class Serializer: 
189class Serializer:
190    """
191    BCS serializer for writing typed values to a byte stream.
192
193    Writes primitive types (integers, strings, bytes) and complex structures
194    in BCS format. The resulting bytes are deterministic and suitable for
195    cryptographic signing.
196
197    Example:
198        >>> ser = Serializer()
199        >>> ser.u64(12345)
200        >>> ser.str("hello")
201        >>> data = ser.output()  # Get the serialized bytes
202    """
203
204    _output: io.BytesIO
205
206    def __init__(self) -> None:
207        self._output = io.BytesIO()
208
209    def output(self) -> bytes:
210        """Get the serialized bytes."""
211        return self._output.getvalue()
212
213    def bool(self, value: bool) -> None:
214        self._write_int(int(value), 1)
215
216    def to_bytes(self, value: bytes) -> None:
217        self.uleb128(len(value))
218        self._output.write(value)
219
220    def fixed_bytes(self, value: bytes) -> None:
221        self._output.write(value)
222
223    def map(
224        self,
225        values: typing.Dict[typing.Any, typing.Any],
226        key_encoder: typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.Any], None],
227        value_encoder: typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.Any], None],
228    ) -> None:
229        encoded_values = []
230        for key, value in values.items():
231            encoded_values.append(
232                (encoder(key, key_encoder), encoder(value, value_encoder))
233            )
234        encoded_values.sort(key=lambda item: item[0])
235
236        self.uleb128(len(encoded_values))
237        for key, value in encoded_values:
238            self.fixed_bytes(key)
239            self.fixed_bytes(value)
240
241    @staticmethod
242    def sequence_serializer(
243        value_encoder: typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.Any], None],
244    ) -> typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.List[typing.Any]], None]:
245        return lambda self, values: self.sequence(values, value_encoder)
246
247    def sequence(
248        self,
249        values: typing.List[typing.Any],
250        value_encoder: typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.Any], None],
251    ) -> None:
252        self.uleb128(len(values))
253        for value in values:
254            self.fixed_bytes(encoder(value, value_encoder))
255
256    def str(self, value: str) -> None:
257        self.to_bytes(value.encode())
258
259    def struct(self, value: typing.Any) -> None:
260        value.serialize(self)
261
262    def u8(self, value: int) -> None:
263        if value > MAX_U8:
264            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u8")
265
266        self._write_int(value, 1)
267
268    def u16(self, value: int) -> None:
269        if value > MAX_U16:
270            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u16")
271
272        self._write_int(value, 2)
273
274    def u32(self, value: int) -> None:
275        if value > MAX_U32:
276            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u32")
277
278        self._write_int(value, 4)
279
280    def u64(self, value: int) -> None:
281        if value > MAX_U64:
282            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u64")
283
284        self._write_int(value, 8)
285
286    def u128(self, value: int) -> None:
287        if value > MAX_U128:
288            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u128")
289
290        self._write_int(value, 16)
291
292    def u256(self, value: int) -> None:
293        if value > MAX_U256:
294            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u256")
295
296        self._write_int(value, 32)
297
298    def uleb128(self, value: int) -> None:
299        if value > MAX_U32:
300            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into uleb128")
301
302        while value >= 0x80:
303            # Write 7 (lowest) bits of data and set the 8th bit to 1.
304            byte = value & 0x7F
305            self.u8(byte | 0x80)
306            value >>= 7
307
308        # Write the remaining bits of data and set the highest bit to 0.
309        self.u8(value & 0x7F)
310
311    def _write_int(self, value: int, length: int) -> None:
312        self._output.write(value.to_bytes(length, "little", signed=False))

BCS serializer for writing typed values to a byte stream.

Writes primitive types (integers, strings, bytes) and complex structures in BCS format. The resulting bytes are deterministic and suitable for cryptographic signing.

Example:

ser = Serializer() ser.u64(12345) ser.str("hello") data = ser.output() # Get the serialized bytes

def output(self) -> bytes: 
209    def output(self) -> bytes:
210        """Get the serialized bytes."""
211        return self._output.getvalue()

Get the serialized bytes.

def bool(self, value: <function Serializer.bool>) -> None: 
213    def bool(self, value: bool) -> None:
214        self._write_int(int(value), 1)
def to_bytes(self, value: bytes) -> None: 
216    def to_bytes(self, value: bytes) -> None:
217        self.uleb128(len(value))
218        self._output.write(value)
def fixed_bytes(self, value: bytes) -> None: 
220    def fixed_bytes(self, value: bytes) -> None:
221        self._output.write(value)
def map( self, values: Dict[Any, Any], key_encoder: Callable[[Serializer, Any], NoneType], value_encoder: Callable[[Serializer, Any], NoneType]) -> None: 
223    def map(
224        self,
225        values: typing.Dict[typing.Any, typing.Any],
226        key_encoder: typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.Any], None],
227        value_encoder: typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.Any], None],
228    ) -> None:
229        encoded_values = []
230        for key, value in values.items():
231            encoded_values.append(
232                (encoder(key, key_encoder), encoder(value, value_encoder))
233            )
234        encoded_values.sort(key=lambda item: item[0])
235
236        self.uleb128(len(encoded_values))
237        for key, value in encoded_values:
238            self.fixed_bytes(key)
239            self.fixed_bytes(value)
@staticmethod
def sequence_serializer( value_encoder: Callable[[Serializer, Any], NoneType]) -> Callable[[Serializer, List[Any]], NoneType]: 
241    @staticmethod
242    def sequence_serializer(
243        value_encoder: typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.Any], None],
244    ) -> typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.List[typing.Any]], None]:
245        return lambda self, values: self.sequence(values, value_encoder)
def sequence( self, values: List[Any], value_encoder: Callable[[Serializer, Any], NoneType]) -> None: 
247    def sequence(
248        self,
249        values: typing.List[typing.Any],
250        value_encoder: typing.Callable[[Serializer, typing.Any], None],
251    ) -> None:
252        self.uleb128(len(values))
253        for value in values:
254            self.fixed_bytes(encoder(value, value_encoder))
def str(self, value: <function Serializer.str>) -> None: 
256    def str(self, value: str) -> None:
257        self.to_bytes(value.encode())
def struct(self, value: Any) -> None: 
259    def struct(self, value: typing.Any) -> None:
260        value.serialize(self)
def u8(self, value: int) -> None: 
262    def u8(self, value: int) -> None:
263        if value > MAX_U8:
264            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u8")
265
266        self._write_int(value, 1)
def u16(self, value: int) -> None: 
268    def u16(self, value: int) -> None:
269        if value > MAX_U16:
270            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u16")
271
272        self._write_int(value, 2)
def u32(self, value: int) -> None: 
274    def u32(self, value: int) -> None:
275        if value > MAX_U32:
276            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u32")
277
278        self._write_int(value, 4)
def u64(self, value: int) -> None: 
280    def u64(self, value: int) -> None:
281        if value > MAX_U64:
282            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u64")
283
284        self._write_int(value, 8)
def u128(self, value: int) -> None: 
286    def u128(self, value: int) -> None:
287        if value > MAX_U128:
288            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u128")
289
290        self._write_int(value, 16)
def u256(self, value: int) -> None: 
292    def u256(self, value: int) -> None:
293        if value > MAX_U256:
294            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into u256")
295
296        self._write_int(value, 32)
def uleb128(self, value: int) -> None: 
298    def uleb128(self, value: int) -> None:
299        if value > MAX_U32:
300            raise SerializationError(f"Cannot encode {value} into uleb128")
301
302        while value >= 0x80:
303            # Write 7 (lowest) bits of data and set the 8th bit to 1.
304            byte = value & 0x7F
305            self.u8(byte | 0x80)
306            value >>= 7
307
308        # Write the remaining bits of data and set the highest bit to 0.
309        self.u8(value & 0x7F)
class StructTag(aptos_sdk.bcs.Deserializable, aptos_sdk.bcs.Serializable): 
306class StructTag(Deserializable, Serializable):
307    address: AccountAddress
308    module: str
309    name: str
310    type_args: List[TypeTag]
311
312    def __init__(
313        self,
314        address: AccountAddress,
315        module: str,
316        name: str,
317        type_args: List[TypeTag],
318    ) -> None:
319        self.address = address
320        self.module = module
321        self.name = name
322        self.type_args = type_args
323
324    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
325        if not isinstance(other, StructTag):
326            return NotImplemented
327        return (
328            self.address == other.address
329            and self.module == other.module
330            and self.name == other.name
331            and self.type_args == other.type_args
332        )
333
334    def __hash__(self) -> int:
335        return hash((self.address, self.module, self.name, tuple(self.type_args)))
336
337    def __str__(self) -> str:
338        value = f"{self.address}::{self.module}::{self.name}"
339        if len(self.type_args) > 0:
340            value += f"<{self.type_args[0]}"
341            for type_arg in self.type_args[1:]:
342                value += f", {type_arg}"
343            value += ">"
344        return value
345
346    @staticmethod
347    def from_str(type_tag: str) -> StructTag:
348        return StructTag._from_str_internal(type_tag, 0)[0][0].value
349
350    @staticmethod
351    def _from_str_internal(type_tag: str, index: int) -> Tuple[List[TypeTag], int]:
352        name = ""
353        tags: List[TypeTag] = []
354        inner_tags: List[TypeTag] = []
355
356        while index < len(type_tag):
357            letter = type_tag[index]
358            index += 1
359
360            if letter == " ":
361                continue
362
363            if letter == "<":
364                (inner_tags, index) = StructTag._from_str_internal(type_tag, index)
365            elif letter == ",":
366                split = name.split("::")
367                tag = TypeTag(
368                    StructTag(
369                        AccountAddress.from_str_relaxed(split[0]),
370                        split[1],
371                        split[2],
372                        inner_tags,
373                    )
374                )
375                tags.append(tag)
376                name = ""
377                inner_tags = []
378            elif letter == ">":
379                break
380            else:
381                name += letter
382
383        split = name.split("::")
384        tag = TypeTag(
385            StructTag(
386                AccountAddress.from_str_relaxed(split[0]),
387                split[1],
388                split[2],
389                inner_tags,
390            )
391        )
392        tags.append(tag)
393        return (tags, index)
394
395    def variant(self) -> int:
396        return TypeTag.STRUCT
397
398    @staticmethod
399    def deserialize(deserializer: Deserializer) -> StructTag:
400        address = deserializer.struct(AccountAddress)
401        module = deserializer.str()
402        name = deserializer.str()
403        type_args = deserializer.sequence(TypeTag.deserialize)
404        return StructTag(address, module, name, type_args)
405
406    def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None:
407        self.address.serialize(serializer)
408        serializer.str(self.module)
409        serializer.str(self.name)
410        serializer.sequence(self.type_args, Serializer.struct)

Protocol for types that can be deserialized from BCS bytes.

StructTag( address: AccountAddress, module: str, name: str, type_args: List[TypeTag]) 
312    def __init__(
313        self,
314        address: AccountAddress,
315        module: str,
316        name: str,
317        type_args: List[TypeTag],
318    ) -> None:
319        self.address = address
320        self.module = module
321        self.name = name
322        self.type_args = type_args
address: AccountAddress
module: str
name: str
type_args: List[TypeTag]
@staticmethod
def from_str(type_tag: str) -> StructTag: 
346    @staticmethod
347    def from_str(type_tag: str) -> StructTag:
348        return StructTag._from_str_internal(type_tag, 0)[0][0].value
def variant(self) -> int: 
395    def variant(self) -> int:
396        return TypeTag.STRUCT
@staticmethod
def deserialize(deserializer: Deserializer) -> StructTag: 
398    @staticmethod
399    def deserialize(deserializer: Deserializer) -> StructTag:
400        address = deserializer.struct(AccountAddress)
401        module = deserializer.str()
402        name = deserializer.str()
403        type_args = deserializer.sequence(TypeTag.deserialize)
404        return StructTag(address, module, name, type_args)

Deserialize from a Deserializer stream.

def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None: 
406    def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None:
407        self.address.serialize(serializer)
408        serializer.str(self.module)
409        serializer.str(self.name)
410        serializer.sequence(self.type_args, Serializer.struct)

class TypeTag(aptos_sdk.bcs.Deserializable, aptos_sdk.bcs.Serializable): 
14class TypeTag(Deserializable, Serializable):
15    """TypeTag represents a primitive in Move."""
16
17    BOOL: int = 0
18    U8: int = 1
19    U64: int = 2
20    U128: int = 3
21    ACCOUNT_ADDRESS: int = 4
22    SIGNER: int = 5
23    VECTOR: int = 6
24    STRUCT: int = 7
25    U16: int = 8
26    U32: int = 9
27    U256: int = 10
28
29    value: typing.Any
30
31    def __init__(self, value: typing.Any) -> None:
32        self.value = value
33
34    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
35        if not isinstance(other, TypeTag):
36            return NotImplemented
37        return (
38            self.value.variant() == other.value.variant() and self.value == other.value
39        )
40
41    def __hash__(self) -> int:
42        return hash((self.value.variant(), self.value))
43
44    def __str__(self) -> str:
45        return self.value.__str__()
46
47    def __repr__(self) -> str:
48        return self.__str__()
49
50    @staticmethod
51    def deserialize(deserializer: Deserializer) -> TypeTag:
52        variant = deserializer.uleb128()
53        if variant == TypeTag.BOOL:
54            return TypeTag(BoolTag.deserialize(deserializer))
55        elif variant == TypeTag.U8:
56            return TypeTag(U8Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
57        elif variant == TypeTag.U16:
58            return TypeTag(U16Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
59        elif variant == TypeTag.U32:
60            return TypeTag(U32Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
61        elif variant == TypeTag.U64:
62            return TypeTag(U64Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
63        elif variant == TypeTag.U128:
64            return TypeTag(U128Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
65        elif variant == TypeTag.U256:
66            return TypeTag(U256Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
67        elif variant == TypeTag.ACCOUNT_ADDRESS:
68            return TypeTag(AccountAddressTag.deserialize(deserializer))
69        elif variant == TypeTag.SIGNER:
70            raise NotImplementedError
71        elif variant == TypeTag.VECTOR:
72            raise NotImplementedError
73        elif variant == TypeTag.STRUCT:
74            return TypeTag(StructTag.deserialize(deserializer))
75        raise NotImplementedError
76
77    def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None:
78        serializer.uleb128(self.value.variant())
79        serializer.struct(self.value)

TypeTag represents a primitive in Move.

32        self.value = value

U8: int = 1

STRUCT: int = 7

U256: int = 10
value: Any
@staticmethod
def deserialize(deserializer: Deserializer) -> TypeTag: 
50    @staticmethod
51    def deserialize(deserializer: Deserializer) -> TypeTag:
52        variant = deserializer.uleb128()
53        if variant == TypeTag.BOOL:
54            return TypeTag(BoolTag.deserialize(deserializer))
55        elif variant == TypeTag.U8:
56            return TypeTag(U8Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
57        elif variant == TypeTag.U16:
58            return TypeTag(U16Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
59        elif variant == TypeTag.U32:
60            return TypeTag(U32Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
61        elif variant == TypeTag.U64:
62            return TypeTag(U64Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
63        elif variant == TypeTag.U128:
64            return TypeTag(U128Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
65        elif variant == TypeTag.U256:
66            return TypeTag(U256Tag.deserialize(deserializer))
67        elif variant == TypeTag.ACCOUNT_ADDRESS:
68            return TypeTag(AccountAddressTag.deserialize(deserializer))
69        elif variant == TypeTag.SIGNER:
70            raise NotImplementedError
71        elif variant == TypeTag.VECTOR:
72            raise NotImplementedError
73        elif variant == TypeTag.STRUCT:
74            return TypeTag(StructTag.deserialize(deserializer))
75        raise NotImplementedError

Deserialize from a Deserializer stream.

def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None: 
77    def serialize(self, serializer: Serializer) -> None:
78        serializer.uleb128(self.value.variant())
79        serializer.struct(self.value)

Ed25519PrivateKey = <class 'PrivateKey'>
Ed25519PublicKey = <class 'PublicKey'>
Ed25519Signature = <class 'Signature'>
Ed25519MultiPublicKey = <class 'aptos_sdk.ed25519.MultiPublicKey'>
Ed25519MultiSignature = <class 'aptos_sdk.ed25519.MultiSignature'>
Secp256k1PrivateKey = <class 'PrivateKey'>
Secp256k1PublicKey = <class 'PublicKey'>
Secp256k1Signature = <class 'Signature'>
class PrivateKey(aptos_sdk.bcs.Deserializable, aptos_sdk.bcs.Serializable, typing.Protocol): 
 22class PrivateKey(Deserializable, Serializable, Protocol):
 23    def hex(self) -> str: ...
 24
 25    def public_key(self) -> PublicKey: ...
 26
 27    def sign(self, data: bytes) -> Signature: ...
 28
 29    """
 30    The AIP-80 compliant prefixes for each private key type. Append this to a private key's hex representation
 31    to get an AIP-80 compliant string.
 32
 33    [Read about AIP-80](https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-80.md)
 34    """
 35    AIP80_PREFIXES: dict[PrivateKeyVariant, str] = {
 36        PrivateKeyVariant.Ed25519: "ed25519-priv-",
 37        PrivateKeyVariant.Secp256k1: "secp256k1-priv-",
 38    }
 39
 40    @staticmethod
 41    def format_private_key(
 42        private_key: bytes | str, key_type: PrivateKeyVariant
 43    ) -> str:
 44        """
 45        Format a HexInput to an AIP-80 compliant string.
 46
 47        :param private_key: The hex string or bytes format of the private key.
 48        :param key_type: The private key type.
 49        :return: AIP-80 compliant string.
 50        """
 51        if key_type not in PrivateKey.AIP80_PREFIXES:
 52            raise ValueError(f"Unknown private key type: {key_type}")
 53        aip80_prefix = PrivateKey.AIP80_PREFIXES[key_type]
 54
 55        key_value: str | None = None
 56        if isinstance(private_key, str):
 57            if private_key.startswith(aip80_prefix):
 58                key_value = private_key.split("-")[2]
 59            else:
 60                key_value = private_key
 61        elif isinstance(private_key, bytes):
 62            key_value = f"0x{private_key.hex()}"
 63        else:
 64            raise TypeError("Input value must be a string or bytes.")
 65
 66        return f"{aip80_prefix}{key_value}"
 67
 68    @staticmethod
 69    def parse_hex_input(
 70        value: str | bytes, key_type: PrivateKeyVariant, strict: bool | None = None
 71    ) -> bytes:
 72        """
 73        Parse a HexInput that may be a hex string, bytes, or an AIP-80 compliant string to a byte array.
 74
 75        :param value: A hex string, byte array, or AIP-80 compliant string.
 76        :param key_type: The private key type.
 77        :param strict: If true, the value MUST be compliant with AIP-80.
 78        :return: Parsed private key as bytes.
 79        """
 80        if key_type not in PrivateKey.AIP80_PREFIXES:
 81            raise ValueError(f"Unknown private key type: {key_type}")
 82        aip80_prefix = PrivateKey.AIP80_PREFIXES[key_type]
 83
 84        if isinstance(value, str):
 85            if not strict and not value.startswith(aip80_prefix):
 86                # Non-AIP-80 compliant hex string
 87                if strict is None:
 88                    logger.warning(
 89                        "It is recommended that private keys are AIP-80 compliant "
 90                        "(https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-80.md)."
 91                    )
 92                value = value.removeprefix("0x")
 93                return bytes.fromhex(value)
 94            elif value.startswith(aip80_prefix):
 95                # AIP-80 compliant string
 96                value = value.split("-")[2]
 97                value = value.removeprefix("0x")
 98                return bytes.fromhex(value)
 99            else:
100                if strict:
101                    raise ValueError(
102                        "Invalid HexString input. Must be AIP-80 compliant string."
103                    )
104                raise ValueError("Invalid HexString input.")
105        elif isinstance(value, bytes):
106            return value
107        else:
108            raise TypeError("Input value must be a string or bytes.")

Protocol for types that can be deserialized from BCS bytes.

def hex(self) -> str: 
23    def hex(self) -> str: ...
def public_key(self) -> PublicKey: 
25    def public_key(self) -> PublicKey: ...
def sign(self, data: bytes) -> Signature: 
27    def sign(self, data: bytes) -> Signature: ...
AIP80_PREFIXES: dict[aptos_sdk.asymmetric_crypto.PrivateKeyVariant, str] = {<PrivateKeyVariant.Ed25519: 'ed25519'>: 'ed25519-priv-', <PrivateKeyVariant.Secp256k1: 'secp256k1'>: 'secp256k1-priv-'}
@staticmethod
def format_private_key( private_key: bytes | str, key_type: aptos_sdk.asymmetric_crypto.PrivateKeyVariant) -> str: 
40    @staticmethod
41    def format_private_key(
42        private_key: bytes | str, key_type: PrivateKeyVariant
43    ) -> str:
44        """
45        Format a HexInput to an AIP-80 compliant string.
46
47        :param private_key: The hex string or bytes format of the private key.
48        :param key_type: The private key type.
49        :return: AIP-80 compliant string.
50        """
51        if key_type not in PrivateKey.AIP80_PREFIXES:
52            raise ValueError(f"Unknown private key type: {key_type}")
53        aip80_prefix = PrivateKey.AIP80_PREFIXES[key_type]
54
55        key_value: str | None = None
56        if isinstance(private_key, str):
57            if private_key.startswith(aip80_prefix):
58                key_value = private_key.split("-")[2]
59            else:
60                key_value = private_key
61        elif isinstance(private_key, bytes):
62            key_value = f"0x{private_key.hex()}"
63        else:
64            raise TypeError("Input value must be a string or bytes.")
65
66        return f"{aip80_prefix}{key_value}"

Format a HexInput to an AIP-80 compliant string.

Parameters
  • private_key: The hex string or bytes format of the private key.
  • key_type: The private key type.
Returns

AIP-80 compliant string.

@staticmethod
def parse_hex_input( value: str | bytes, key_type: aptos_sdk.asymmetric_crypto.PrivateKeyVariant, strict: bool | None = None) -> bytes: 
 68    @staticmethod
 69    def parse_hex_input(
 70        value: str | bytes, key_type: PrivateKeyVariant, strict: bool | None = None
 71    ) -> bytes:
 72        """
 73        Parse a HexInput that may be a hex string, bytes, or an AIP-80 compliant string to a byte array.
 74
 75        :param value: A hex string, byte array, or AIP-80 compliant string.
 76        :param key_type: The private key type.
 77        :param strict: If true, the value MUST be compliant with AIP-80.
 78        :return: Parsed private key as bytes.
 79        """
 80        if key_type not in PrivateKey.AIP80_PREFIXES:
 81            raise ValueError(f"Unknown private key type: {key_type}")
 82        aip80_prefix = PrivateKey.AIP80_PREFIXES[key_type]
 83
 84        if isinstance(value, str):
 85            if not strict and not value.startswith(aip80_prefix):
 86                # Non-AIP-80 compliant hex string
 87                if strict is None:
 88                    logger.warning(
 89                        "It is recommended that private keys are AIP-80 compliant "
 90                        "(https://github.com/aptos-foundation/AIPs/blob/main/aips/aip-80.md)."
 91                    )
 92                value = value.removeprefix("0x")
 93                return bytes.fromhex(value)
 94            elif value.startswith(aip80_prefix):
 95                # AIP-80 compliant string
 96                value = value.split("-")[2]
 97                value = value.removeprefix("0x")
 98                return bytes.fromhex(value)
 99            else:
100                if strict:
101                    raise ValueError(
102                        "Invalid HexString input. Must be AIP-80 compliant string."
103                    )
104                raise ValueError("Invalid HexString input.")
105        elif isinstance(value, bytes):
106            return value
107        else:
108            raise TypeError("Input value must be a string or bytes.")

Parse a HexInput that may be a hex string, bytes, or an AIP-80 compliant string to a byte array.

Parameters
  • value: A hex string, byte array, or AIP-80 compliant string.
  • key_type: The private key type.
  • strict: If true, the value MUST be compliant with AIP-80.
Returns

Parsed private key as bytes.

class PublicKey(aptos_sdk.bcs.Deserializable, aptos_sdk.bcs.Serializable, typing.Protocol): 
111class PublicKey(Deserializable, Serializable, Protocol):
112    def to_crypto_bytes(self) -> bytes:
113        """
114        A long time ago, someone decided that we should have both bcs and a special representation
115        for MultiEd25519, so we use this to let keys self-define a special encoding.
116        """
117        ...
118
119    def verify(self, data: bytes, signature: Signature) -> bool: ...

Protocol for types that can be deserialized from BCS bytes.

113        """
114        A long time ago, someone decided that we should have both bcs and a special representation
115        for MultiEd25519, so we use this to let keys self-define a special encoding.
116        """
117        ...

A long time ago, someone decided that we should have both bcs and a special representation for MultiEd25519, so we use this to let keys self-define a special encoding.

def verify( self, data: bytes, signature: Signature) -> bool:
