165        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
166        :return: The authentication key and sequence number for the specified address.
167        """
168        response = await self._get(
169            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}",
170            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
171        )
172        if response.status_code >= 400:
173            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
174        return response.json()
175
176    async def account_balance(
177        self,
178        account_address: AccountAddress,
179        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
180        coin_type: Optional[str] = None,
181    ) -> int:
182        """
183        Fetch the Aptos coin balance associated with the account.
184
185        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
186        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
187        :param coin_type: Coin type to get balance for, defaults to "0x1::aptos_coin::AptosCoin".
188        :return: The Aptos coin balance associated with the account
189        """
190        coin_type = coin_type or "0x1::aptos_coin::AptosCoin"
191        result = await self.view_bcs_payload(
192            "0x1::coin",
193            "balance",
194            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str(coin_type))],
195            [TransactionArgument(account_address, Serializer.struct)],
196            ledger_version,
197        )
198        return int(result[0])
199
200    async def account_sequence_number(
201        self, account_address: AccountAddress, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None
202    ) -> int:
203        """
204        Fetch the current sequence number for an account address.
205
206        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
207        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
208        :return: The current sequence number for the specified address.
209        """
210        try:
211            account_res = await self.account(account_address, ledger_version)
212            return int(account_res["sequence_number"])
213        except ApiError as ae:
214            if ae.status_code != 404:
215                raise
216            return 0
217
218    async def account_resource(
219        self,
220        account_address: AccountAddress,
221        resource_type: str,
222        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
223    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
224        """
225        Retrieves an individual resource from a given account and at a specific ledger version.
226
227        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
228        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
229
230        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
231        :param resource_type: Name of struct to retrieve e.g. 0x1::account::Account.
232        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
233        :return: An individual resource from a given account and at a specific ledger version.
234        """
235        response = await self._get(
236            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/resource/{resource_type}",
237            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
238        )
239        if response.status_code == 404:
240            raise ResourceNotFound(resource_type, resource_type)
241        if response.status_code >= 400:
242            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
243        return response.json()
244
245    async def account_resources(
246        self,
247        account_address: AccountAddress,
248        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
249    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
250        """
251        Retrieves all account resources for a given account and a specific ledger version.
252
253        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
254        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
255
256        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
257        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
258        :return: All account resources for a given account and a specific ledger version.
259        """
260        response = await self._get(
261            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/resources",
262            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
263        )
264        if response.status_code == 404:
265            raise AccountNotFound(f"{account_address}", account_address)
266        if response.status_code >= 400:
267            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
268        return response.json()
269
270    async def account_module(
271        self,
272        account_address: AccountAddress,
273        module_name: str,
274        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
275    ) -> dict:
276        """
277        Retrieves an individual module from a given account and at a specific ledger version.
278
279        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
280        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
281
282        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
283        :param module_name: Name of module to retrieve e.g. 'coin'
284        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
285        :return: An individual module from a given account and at a specific ledger version
286        """
287        response = await self._get(
288            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/module/{module_name}",
289            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
290        )
291        if response.status_code >= 400:
292            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
293
294        return response.json()
295
296    async def account_modules(
297        self,
298        account_address: AccountAddress,
299        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
300        limit: Optional[int] = None,
301        start: Optional[str] = None,
302    ) -> dict:
303        """
304        Retrieves all account modules' bytecode for a given account at a specific ledger version.
305
306        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
307        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
308
309        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
310        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
311        :param limit: Max number of account modules to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
312        :param start: Cursor specifying where to start for pagination.
313        :return: All account modules' bytecode for a given account at a specific ledger version.
314        """
315        response = await self._get(
316            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/modules",
317            params={
318                "ledger_version": ledger_version,
319                "limit": limit,
320                "start": start,
321            },
322        )
323        if response.status_code == 404:
324            raise AccountNotFound(f"{account_address}", account_address)
325        if response.status_code >= 400:
326            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
327
328        return response.json()
329
330    #
331    # Blocks
332    #
333
334    async def blocks_by_height(
335        self,
336        block_height: int,
337        with_transactions: bool = False,
338    ) -> dict:
339        """
340        Fetch the transactions in a block and the corresponding block information.
341
342        Transactions are limited by max default transactions size. If not all transactions are present, the user will
343        need to query for the rest of the transactions via the get transactions API. If the block is pruned, it will
344        return a 410.
345
346        :param block_height: Block height to lookup. Starts at 0.
347        :param with_transactions: If set to true, include all transactions in the block.
348        :returns: Block information.
349        """
350        response = await self._get(
351            endpoint=f"blocks/by_height/{block_height}",
352            params={
353                "with_transactions": with_transactions,
354            },
355        )
356        if response.status_code >= 400:
357            raise ApiError(f"{response.text}", response.status_code)
358
359        return response.json()
360
361    async def blocks_by_version(
362        self,
363        version: int,
364        with_transactions: bool = False,
365    ) -> dict:
366        """
367        Fetch the transactions in a block and the corresponding block information, given a version in the block.
368
369        Transactions are limited by max default transactions size. If not all transactions are present, the user will
370        need to query for the rest of the transactions via the get transactions API. If the block is pruned, it will
371        return a 410.
372
373        :param version: Ledger version to lookup block information for.
374        :param with_transactions: If set to true, include all transactions in the block.
375        :returns: Block information.
376        """
377        response = await self._get(
378            endpoint=f"blocks/by_version/{version}",
379            params={
380                "with_transactions": with_transactions,
381            },
382        )
383        if response.status_code >= 400:
384            raise ApiError(f"{response.text}", response.status_code)
385
386        return response.json()
387
388    #
389    # Events
390    #
391
392    async def event_by_creation_number(
393        self,
394        account_address: AccountAddress,
395        creation_number: int,
396        limit: Optional[int] = None,
397        start: Optional[int] = None,
398    ) -> List[dict]:
399        """
400        Retrieve events corresponding to an account address and creation number indicating the event type emitted
401        to that account.
402
403        Creation numbers are monotonically increasing for each account address.
404
405        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
406        :param creation_number: Creation number corresponding to the event stream originating from the given account.
407        :param limit: Max number of events to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
408        :param start: Starting sequence number of events.If unspecified, by default will retrieve the most recent.
409        :returns: Events corresponding to an account address and creation number indicating the event type emitted
410        to that account.
411        """
412        response = await self._get(
413            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/events/{creation_number}",
414            params={
415                "limit": limit,
416                "start": start,
417            },
418        )
419        if response.status_code >= 400:
420            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
421
422        return response.json()
423
424    async def events_by_event_handle(
425        self,
426        account_address: AccountAddress,
427        event_handle: str,
428        field_name: str,
429        limit: Optional[int] = None,
430        start: Optional[int] = None,
431    ) -> List[dict]:
432        """
433        Retrieve events corresponding to an account address, event handle (struct name) and field name.
434
435        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
436        :param event_handle: Name of struct to lookup event handle e.g., '0x1::account::Account'.
437        :param field_name: Name of field to lookup event handle e.g., 'withdraw_events'
438        :param limit: Max number of events to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
439        :param start: Starting sequence number of events.If unspecified, by default will retrieve the most recent.
440        :returns: Events corresponding to the provided account address, event handle and field name.
441        """
442        response = await self._get(
443            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/events/{event_handle}/{field_name}",
444            params={
445                "limit": limit,
446                "start": start,
447            },
448        )
449        if response.status_code >= 400:
450            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
451
452        return response.json()
453
454    async def current_timestamp(self) -> float:
455        info = await self.info()
456        return float(info["ledger_timestamp"]) / 1_000_000
457
458    async def get_table_item(
459        self,
460        handle: str,
461        key_type: str,
462        value_type: str,
463        key: Any,
464        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
465    ) -> Any:
466        response = await self._post(
467            endpoint=f"tables/{handle}/item",
468            data={
469                "key_type": key_type,
470                "value_type": value_type,
471                "key": key,
472            },
473            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
474        )
475        if response.status_code >= 400:
476            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
477        return response.json()
478
479    async def aggregator_value(
480        self,
481        account_address: AccountAddress,
482        resource_type: str,
483        aggregator_path: List[str],
484    ) -> int:
485        source = await self.account_resource(account_address, resource_type)
486        source_data = data = source["data"]
487
488        while len(aggregator_path) > 0:
489            key = aggregator_path.pop()
490            if key not in data:
491                raise ApiError(
492                    f"aggregator path not found in data: {source_data}", source_data
493                )
494            data = data[key]
495
496        if "vec" not in data:
497            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
498        data = data["vec"]
499        if len(data) != 1:
500            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
501        data = data[0]
502        if "aggregator" not in data:
503            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
504        data = data["aggregator"]
505        if "vec" not in data:
506            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
507        data = data["vec"]
508        if len(data) != 1:
509            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
510        data = data[0]
511        if "handle" not in data:
512            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
513        if "key" not in data:
514            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
515        handle = data["handle"]
516        key = data["key"]
517        return int(await self.get_table_item(handle, "address", "u128", key))
518
519    #
520    # Ledger accessors
521    #
522
523    async def info(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
524        response = await self.client.get(self.base_url)
525        if response.status_code >= 400:
526            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
527        return response.json()
528
529    #
530    # Transactions
531    #
532
533    async def simulate_bcs_transaction(
534        self,
535        signed_transaction: SignedTransaction,
536        estimate_gas_usage: bool = False,
537    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
538        headers = {"Content-Type": "application/x.aptos.signed_transaction+bcs"}
539        params = {}
540        if estimate_gas_usage:
541            params = {
542                "estimate_gas_unit_price": "true",
543                "estimate_max_gas_amount": "true",
544            }
545
546        response = await self.client.post(
547            f"{self.base_url}/transactions/simulate",
548            params=params,
549            headers=headers,
550            content=signed_transaction.bytes(),
551        )
552        if response.status_code >= 400:
553            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
554
555        return response.json()
556
557    async def simulate_transaction(
558        self,
559        transaction: RawTransaction,
560        sender: Account,
561        estimate_gas_usage: bool = False,
562    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
563        # Note that simulated transactions are not signed and have all 0 signatures!
564        authenticator = sender.sign_simulated_transaction(transaction)
565        return await self.simulate_bcs_transaction(
566            signed_transaction=SignedTransaction(transaction, authenticator),
567            estimate_gas_usage=estimate_gas_usage,
568        )
569
570    async def submit_bcs_transaction(
571        self, signed_transaction: SignedTransaction
572    ) -> str:
573        headers = {"Content-Type": "application/x.aptos.signed_transaction+bcs"}
574        response = await self.client.post(
575            f"{self.base_url}/transactions",
576            headers=headers,
577            content=signed_transaction.bytes(),
578        )
579        if response.status_code >= 400:
580            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
581        return response.json()["hash"]
582
583    async def submit_and_wait_for_bcs_transaction(
584        self, signed_transaction: SignedTransaction
585    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
586        txn_hash = await self.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
587        await self.wait_for_transaction(txn_hash)
588        return await self.transaction_by_hash(txn_hash)
589
590    async def transaction_pending(self, txn_hash: str) -> bool:
591        response = await self._get(endpoint=f"transactions/by_hash/{txn_hash}")
592        # TODO(@davidiw): consider raising a different error here, since this is an ambiguous state
593        if response.status_code == 404:
594            return True
595        if response.status_code >= 400:
596            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
597        return response.json()["type"] == "pending_transaction"
598
599    async def wait_for_transaction(self, txn_hash: str) -> None:
600        """
601        Wait for a transaction to be confirmed on chain.
602
603        Waits up to the duration specified in client_config for a transaction
604        to move past pending state.
605
606        Args:
607            txn_hash: The transaction hash to wait for.
608
609        Raises:
610            TransactionTimeout: If the transaction is not confirmed within the timeout.
611            TransactionFailed: If the transaction was executed but failed.
612        """
613        from .errors import TransactionFailed, TransactionTimeout
614
615        count = 0
616        while await self.transaction_pending(txn_hash):
617            if count >= self.client_config.transaction_wait_in_seconds:
618                raise TransactionTimeout(
619                    txn_hash, self.client_config.transaction_wait_in_seconds
620                )
621            await asyncio.sleep(1)
622            count += 1
623
624        response = await self._get(endpoint=f"transactions/by_hash/{txn_hash}")
625        result = response.json()
626        if "success" not in result or not result["success"]:
627            vm_status = result.get("vm_status", "unknown")
628            raise TransactionFailed(txn_hash, vm_status)
629
630    async def account_transaction_sequence_number_status(
631        self, address: AccountAddress, sequence_number: int
632    ) -> bool:
633        """Retrieve the state of a transaction by account and sequence number."""
634        response = await self._get(
635            endpoint=f"accounts/{address}/transactions",
636            params={
637                "limit": 1,
638                "start": sequence_number,
639            },
640        )
641        if response.status_code >= 400:
642            logging.info(f"k {response}")
643            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
644        data = response.json()
645        return len(data) == 1 and data[0]["type"] != "pending_transaction"
646
647    async def transaction_by_hash(self, txn_hash: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
648        response = await self._get(endpoint=f"transactions/by_hash/{txn_hash}")
649        if response.status_code >= 400:
650            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
651        return response.json()
652
653    async def transaction_by_version(self, version: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
654        response = await self._get(endpoint=f"transactions/by_version/{version}")
655        if response.status_code >= 400:
656            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
657        return response.json()
658
659    async def transactions_by_account(
660        self,
661        account_address: AccountAddress,
662        limit: Optional[int] = None,
663        start: Optional[int] = None,
664    ) -> List[dict]:
665        """
666        Retrieves on-chain committed transactions from an account.
667
668        If the start version is too far in the past, a 410 will be returned. If no start version is given, it will
669        start at version 0.
670
671        To retrieve a pending transaction, use /transactions/by_hash.
672
673        :param account_address: Address of account with or without a 0x prefix.
674        :param limit: Max number of transactions to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
675        :param start: Account sequence number to start list of transactions. Defaults to latest transactions.
676        :returns: List of on-chain committed transactions from the specified account.
677        """
678        response = await self._get(
679            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/transactions",
680            params={
681                "limit": limit,
682                "start": start,
683            },
684        )
685        if response.status_code >= 400:
686            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
687
688        return response.json()
689
690    async def transactions(
691        self,
692        limit: Optional[int] = None,
693        start: Optional[int] = None,
694    ) -> List[dict]:
695        """
696        Retrieve on-chain committed transactions.
697
698        The page size and start ledger version can be provided to get a specific sequence of transactions. If the
699        version has been pruned, then a 410 will be returned. To retrieve a pending transaction,
700        use /transactions/by_hash.
701
702        :param limit: Max number of transactions to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
703        :param start: Ledger version to start list of transactions. Defaults to showing the latest transactions.
704        """
705        response = await self._get(
706            endpoint="transactions",
707            params={
708                "limit": limit,
709                "start": start,
710            },
711        )
712        if response.status_code >= 400:
713            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
714
715        return response.json()
716
717    #
718    # Transaction helpers
719    #
720
721    async def create_multi_agent_bcs_transaction(
722        self,
723        sender: Account,
724        secondary_accounts: List[Account],
725        payload: TransactionPayload,
726    ) -> SignedTransaction:
727        raw_transaction = MultiAgentRawTransaction(
728            RawTransaction(
729                sender.address(),
730                await self.account_sequence_number(sender.address()),
731                payload,
732                self.client_config.max_gas_amount,
733                self.client_config.gas_unit_price,
734                int(time.time()) + self.client_config.expiration_ttl,
735                await self.chain_id(),
736            ),
737            [x.address() for x in secondary_accounts],
738        )
739
740        authenticator = Authenticator(
741            MultiAgentAuthenticator(
742                sender.sign_transaction(raw_transaction),
743                [
744                    (
745                        x.address(),
746                        x.sign_transaction(raw_transaction),
747                    )
748                    for x in secondary_accounts
749                ],
750            )
751        )
752
753        return SignedTransaction(raw_transaction.inner(), authenticator)
754
755    async def create_bcs_transaction(
756        self,
757        sender: Account | AccountAddress,
758        payload: TransactionPayload,
759        sequence_number: Optional[int] = None,
760    ) -> RawTransaction:
761        if isinstance(sender, Account):
762            sender_address = sender.address()
763        else:
764            sender_address = sender
765
766        sequence_number = (
767            sequence_number
768            if sequence_number is not None
769            else await self.account_sequence_number(sender_address)
770        )
771        return RawTransaction(
772            sender_address,
773            sequence_number,
774            payload,
775            self.client_config.max_gas_amount,
776            self.client_config.gas_unit_price,
777            int(time.time()) + self.client_config.expiration_ttl,
778            await self.chain_id(),
779        )
780
781    async def create_bcs_signed_transaction(
782        self,
783        sender: Account,
784        payload: TransactionPayload,
785        sequence_number: Optional[int] = None,
786    ) -> SignedTransaction:
787        raw_transaction = await self.create_bcs_transaction(
788            sender, payload, sequence_number
789        )
790        authenticator = sender.sign_transaction(raw_transaction)
791        return SignedTransaction(raw_transaction, authenticator)
792
793    #
794    # Transaction wrappers
795    #
796
797    # :!:>bcs_transfer
798    async def bcs_transfer(
799        self,
800        sender: Account,
801        recipient: AccountAddress,
802        amount: int,
803        sequence_number: Optional[int] = None,
804    ) -> str:
805        transaction_arguments = [
806            TransactionArgument(recipient, Serializer.struct),
807            TransactionArgument(amount, Serializer.u64),
808        ]
809
810        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
811            "0x1::aptos_account",
812            "transfer",
813            [],
814            transaction_arguments,
815        )
816
817        signed_transaction = await self.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
818            sender, TransactionPayload(payload), sequence_number=sequence_number
819        )
820        return await self.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)  # <:!:bcs_transfer
821
822    async def transfer_coins(
823        self,
824        sender: Account,
825        recipient: AccountAddress,
826        coin_type: str,
827        amount: int,
828        sequence_number: Optional[int] = None,
829    ) -> str:
830        transaction_arguments = [
831            TransactionArgument(recipient, Serializer.struct),
832            TransactionArgument(amount, Serializer.u64),
833        ]
834
835        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
836            "0x1::aptos_account",
837            "transfer_coins",
838            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str(coin_type))],
839            transaction_arguments,
840        )
841
842        signed_transaction = await self.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
843            sender, TransactionPayload(payload), sequence_number=sequence_number
844        )
845        return await self.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
846
847    async def transfer_object(
848        self, owner: Account, object: AccountAddress, to: AccountAddress
849    ) -> str:
850        transaction_arguments = [
851            TransactionArgument(object, Serializer.struct),
852            TransactionArgument(to, Serializer.struct),
853        ]
854
855        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
856            "0x1::object",
857            "transfer_call",
858            [],
859            transaction_arguments,
860        )
861
862        signed_transaction = await self.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
863            owner,
864            TransactionPayload(payload),
865        )
866        return await self.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
867
868    async def view(
869        self,
870        function: str,
871        type_arguments: List[str],
872        arguments: List[str],
873        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
874    ) -> bytes:
875        """
876        Execute a view Move function with the given parameters and return its execution result.
877
878        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
879        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
880
881        :param function: Entry function id is string representation of an entry function defined on-chain.
882        :param type_arguments: Type arguments of the function.
883        :param arguments: Arguments of the function.
884        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
885        :returns: Execution result.
886        """
887        response = await self._post(
888            endpoint="view",
889            params={
890                "ledger_version": ledger_version,
891            },
892            headers={
893                "Accept": "application/json",
894                "Content-Type": "application/json",
895            },
896            data={
897                "function": function,
898                "type_arguments": type_arguments,
899                "arguments": arguments,
900            },
901        )
902        if response.status_code >= 400:
903            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
904
905        return response.content
906
907    async def view_bcs_payload(
908        self,
909        module: str,
910        function: str,
911        ty_args: List[TypeTag],
912        args: List[TransactionArgument],
913        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
914    ) -> Any:
915        """
916        Execute a view Move function with the given parameters and return its execution result.
917        Note, this differs from `view` as in this expects bcs compatible inputs and submits the
918        view function in bcs format. This is convenient for clients that execute functions in
919        transactions similar to view functions.
920
921        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
922        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
923
924        :param function: Entry function id is string representation of an entry function defined on-chain.
925        :param type_arguments: Type arguments of the function.
926        :param arguments: Arguments of the function.
927        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
928        :returns: Execution result.
929        """
930        request = f"{self.base_url}/view"
931        if ledger_version:
932            request = f"{request}?ledger_version={ledger_version}"
933
934        view_data = EntryFunction.natural(module, function, ty_args, args)
935        ser = Serializer()
936        view_data.serialize(ser)
937        headers = {"Content-Type": "application/x.aptos.view_function+bcs"}
938        response = await self.client.post(
939            request, headers=headers, content=ser.output()
940        )
941        if response.status_code >= 400:
942            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
943        return response.json()
944
945    async def _post(
946        self,
947        endpoint: str,
948        params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
949        headers: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
950        data: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
951    ) -> httpx.Response:
952        # format params:
953        params = {} if params is None else params
954        params = {key: val for key, val in params.items() if val is not None}
955        return await self.client.post(
956            url=f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint}",
957            params=params,
958            headers=headers,
959            json=data,
960        )
961
962    async def _get(
963        self, endpoint: str, params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
964    ) -> httpx.Response:
965        # format params:
966        params = {} if params is None else params
967        params = {key: val for key, val in params.items() if val is not None}
968        return await self.client.get(
969            url=f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint}",
970            params=params,
971        )

A wrapper around the Aptos-core REST API.

This is the main entry point for interacting with an Aptos fullnode. It provides methods for querying accounts, submitting transactions, and reading on-chain data.

Supports async context manager for automatic resource cleanup:

async with RestClient("https://fullnode.testnet.aptoslabs.com/v1") as client:
    balance = await client.account_balance(address)

Attributes: base_url: The fullnode REST API URL. client_config: Configuration options for the client. client: The underlying httpx AsyncClient.

RestClient( base_url: str, client_config: ClientConfig = ClientConfig(expiration_ttl=600, gas_unit_price=100, max_gas_amount=100000, transaction_wait_in_seconds=20, http2=True, api_key=None)) 
108    def __init__(self, base_url: str, client_config: ClientConfig = ClientConfig()):
109        self.base_url = base_url
110        # Default limits
111        limits = httpx.Limits()
112        # Default timeouts but do not set a pool timeout, since the idea is that jobs will wait as
113        # long as progress is being made.
114        timeout = httpx.Timeout(60.0, pool=None)
115        # Default headers
116        headers = {Metadata.APTOS_HEADER: Metadata.get_aptos_header_val()}
117        self.client = httpx.AsyncClient(
118            http2=client_config.http2,
119            limits=limits,
120            timeout=timeout,
121            headers=headers,
122        )
123        self.client_config = client_config
124        self._chain_id = None
125        self._used_context_manager = False
126        if client_config.api_key:
127            self.client.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {client_config.api_key}"
client: httpx.AsyncClient
client_config: ClientConfig
base_url: str
async def close(self): 
139    async def close(self):
140        """
141        Close the underlying HTTP client.
142
143        This should be called when you're done using the client to release
144        resources. Using the async context manager handles this automatically.
145        """
146        await self.client.aclose()

Close the underlying HTTP client.

This should be called when you're done using the client to release resources. Using the async context manager handles this automatically.

async def chain_id(self): 
148    async def chain_id(self):
149        if not self._chain_id:
150            info = await self.info()
151            self._chain_id = int(info["chain_id"])
152        return self._chain_id
async def account( self, account_address: AccountAddress, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None) -> Dict[str, str]: 
158    async def account(
159        self, account_address: AccountAddress, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None
160    ) -> Dict[str, str]:
161        """
162        Fetch the authentication key and the sequence number for an account address.
163
164        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
165        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
166        :return: The authentication key and sequence number for the specified address.
167        """
168        response = await self._get(
169            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}",
170            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
171        )
172        if response.status_code >= 400:
173            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
174        return response.json()

Fetch the authentication key and the sequence number for an account address.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
Returns

The authentication key and sequence number for the specified address.

async def account_balance( self, account_address: AccountAddress, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None, coin_type: Optional[str] = None) -> int: 
176    async def account_balance(
177        self,
178        account_address: AccountAddress,
179        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
180        coin_type: Optional[str] = None,
181    ) -> int:
182        """
183        Fetch the Aptos coin balance associated with the account.
184
185        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
186        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
187        :param coin_type: Coin type to get balance for, defaults to "0x1::aptos_coin::AptosCoin".
188        :return: The Aptos coin balance associated with the account
189        """
190        coin_type = coin_type or "0x1::aptos_coin::AptosCoin"
191        result = await self.view_bcs_payload(
192            "0x1::coin",
193            "balance",
194            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str(coin_type))],
195            [TransactionArgument(account_address, Serializer.struct)],
196            ledger_version,
197        )
198        return int(result[0])

Fetch the Aptos coin balance associated with the account.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
  • coin_type: Coin type to get balance for, defaults to "0x1::aptos_coin:: AptosCoin".
Returns

The Aptos coin balance associated with the account

async def account_sequence_number( self, account_address: AccountAddress, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None) -> int: 
200    async def account_sequence_number(
201        self, account_address: AccountAddress, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None
202    ) -> int:
203        """
204        Fetch the current sequence number for an account address.
205
206        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
207        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
208        :return: The current sequence number for the specified address.
209        """
210        try:
211            account_res = await self.account(account_address, ledger_version)
212            return int(account_res["sequence_number"])
213        except ApiError as ae:
214            if ae.status_code != 404:
215                raise
216            return 0

Fetch the current sequence number for an account address.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
Returns

The current sequence number for the specified address.

async def account_resource( self, account_address: AccountAddress, resource_type: str, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
218    async def account_resource(
219        self,
220        account_address: AccountAddress,
221        resource_type: str,
222        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
223    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
224        """
225        Retrieves an individual resource from a given account and at a specific ledger version.
226
227        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
228        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
229
230        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
231        :param resource_type: Name of struct to retrieve e.g. 0x1::account::Account.
232        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
233        :return: An individual resource from a given account and at a specific ledger version.
234        """
235        response = await self._get(
236            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/resource/{resource_type}",
237            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
238        )
239        if response.status_code == 404:
240            raise ResourceNotFound(resource_type, resource_type)
241        if response.status_code >= 400:
242            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
243        return response.json()

Retrieves an individual resource from a given account and at a specific ledger version.

The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • resource_type: Name of struct to retrieve e.g. 0x1::account:: Account.
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
Returns

An individual resource from a given account and at a specific ledger version.

async def account_resources( self, account_address: AccountAddress, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: 
245    async def account_resources(
246        self,
247        account_address: AccountAddress,
248        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
249    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
250        """
251        Retrieves all account resources for a given account and a specific ledger version.
252
253        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
254        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
255
256        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
257        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
258        :return: All account resources for a given account and a specific ledger version.
259        """
260        response = await self._get(
261            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/resources",
262            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
263        )
264        if response.status_code == 404:
265            raise AccountNotFound(f"{account_address}", account_address)
266        if response.status_code >= 400:
267            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
268        return response.json()

Retrieves all account resources for a given account and a specific ledger version.

The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
Returns

All account resources for a given account and a specific ledger version.

async def account_module( self, account_address: AccountAddress, module_name: str, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None) -> dict: 
270    async def account_module(
271        self,
272        account_address: AccountAddress,
273        module_name: str,
274        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
275    ) -> dict:
276        """
277        Retrieves an individual module from a given account and at a specific ledger version.
278
279        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
280        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
281
282        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
283        :param module_name: Name of module to retrieve e.g. 'coin'
284        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
285        :return: An individual module from a given account and at a specific ledger version
286        """
287        response = await self._get(
288            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/module/{module_name}",
289            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
290        )
291        if response.status_code >= 400:
292            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
293
294        return response.json()

Retrieves an individual module from a given account and at a specific ledger version.

The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • module_name: Name of module to retrieve e.g. 'coin'
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
Returns

An individual module from a given account and at a specific ledger version

async def account_modules( self, account_address: AccountAddress, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None, limit: Optional[int] = None, start: Optional[str] = None) -> dict: 
296    async def account_modules(
297        self,
298        account_address: AccountAddress,
299        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
300        limit: Optional[int] = None,
301        start: Optional[str] = None,
302    ) -> dict:
303        """
304        Retrieves all account modules' bytecode for a given account at a specific ledger version.
305
306        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
307        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
308
309        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
310        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
311        :param limit: Max number of account modules to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
312        :param start: Cursor specifying where to start for pagination.
313        :return: All account modules' bytecode for a given account at a specific ledger version.
314        """
315        response = await self._get(
316            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/modules",
317            params={
318                "ledger_version": ledger_version,
319                "limit": limit,
320                "start": start,
321            },
322        )
323        if response.status_code == 404:
324            raise AccountNotFound(f"{account_address}", account_address)
325        if response.status_code >= 400:
326            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
327
328        return response.json()

Retrieves all account modules' bytecode for a given account at a specific ledger version.

The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
  • limit: Max number of account modules to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
  • start: Cursor specifying where to start for pagination.
Returns

All account modules' bytecode for a given account at a specific ledger version.

async def blocks_by_height(self, block_height: int, with_transactions: bool = False) -> dict: 
334    async def blocks_by_height(
335        self,
336        block_height: int,
337        with_transactions: bool = False,
338    ) -> dict:
339        """
340        Fetch the transactions in a block and the corresponding block information.
341
342        Transactions are limited by max default transactions size. If not all transactions are present, the user will
343        need to query for the rest of the transactions via the get transactions API. If the block is pruned, it will
344        return a 410.
345
346        :param block_height: Block height to lookup. Starts at 0.
347        :param with_transactions: If set to true, include all transactions in the block.
348        :returns: Block information.
349        """
350        response = await self._get(
351            endpoint=f"blocks/by_height/{block_height}",
352            params={
353                "with_transactions": with_transactions,
354            },
355        )
356        if response.status_code >= 400:
357            raise ApiError(f"{response.text}", response.status_code)
358
359        return response.json()

Fetch the transactions in a block and the corresponding block information.

Transactions are limited by max default transactions size. If not all transactions are present, the user will need to query for the rest of the transactions via the get transactions API. If the block is pruned, it will return a 410.

Parameters
  • block_height: Block height to lookup. Starts at 0.
  • with_transactions: If set to true, include all transactions in the block. :returns: Block information.
async def blocks_by_version(self, version: int, with_transactions: bool = False) -> dict: 
361    async def blocks_by_version(
362        self,
363        version: int,
364        with_transactions: bool = False,
365    ) -> dict:
366        """
367        Fetch the transactions in a block and the corresponding block information, given a version in the block.
368
369        Transactions are limited by max default transactions size. If not all transactions are present, the user will
370        need to query for the rest of the transactions via the get transactions API. If the block is pruned, it will
371        return a 410.
372
373        :param version: Ledger version to lookup block information for.
374        :param with_transactions: If set to true, include all transactions in the block.
375        :returns: Block information.
376        """
377        response = await self._get(
378            endpoint=f"blocks/by_version/{version}",
379            params={
380                "with_transactions": with_transactions,
381            },
382        )
383        if response.status_code >= 400:
384            raise ApiError(f"{response.text}", response.status_code)
385
386        return response.json()

Fetch the transactions in a block and the corresponding block information, given a version in the block.

Transactions are limited by max default transactions size. If not all transactions are present, the user will need to query for the rest of the transactions via the get transactions API. If the block is pruned, it will return a 410.

Parameters
  • version: Ledger version to lookup block information for.
  • with_transactions: If set to true, include all transactions in the block. :returns: Block information.
async def event_by_creation_number( self, account_address: AccountAddress, creation_number: int, limit: Optional[int] = None, start: Optional[int] = None) -> List[dict]: 
392    async def event_by_creation_number(
393        self,
394        account_address: AccountAddress,
395        creation_number: int,
396        limit: Optional[int] = None,
397        start: Optional[int] = None,
398    ) -> List[dict]:
399        """
400        Retrieve events corresponding to an account address and creation number indicating the event type emitted
401        to that account.
402
403        Creation numbers are monotonically increasing for each account address.
404
405        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
406        :param creation_number: Creation number corresponding to the event stream originating from the given account.
407        :param limit: Max number of events to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
408        :param start: Starting sequence number of events.If unspecified, by default will retrieve the most recent.
409        :returns: Events corresponding to an account address and creation number indicating the event type emitted
410        to that account.
411        """
412        response = await self._get(
413            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/events/{creation_number}",
414            params={
415                "limit": limit,
416                "start": start,
417            },
418        )
419        if response.status_code >= 400:
420            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
421
422        return response.json()

Retrieve events corresponding to an account address and creation number indicating the event type emitted to that account.

Creation numbers are monotonically increasing for each account address.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • creation_number: Creation number corresponding to the event stream originating from the given account.
  • limit: Max number of events to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
  • start: Starting sequence number of events.If unspecified, by default will retrieve the most recent. :returns: Events corresponding to an account address and creation number indicating the event type emitted to that account.
async def events_by_event_handle( self, account_address: AccountAddress, event_handle: str, field_name: str, limit: Optional[int] = None, start: Optional[int] = None) -> List[dict]: 
424    async def events_by_event_handle(
425        self,
426        account_address: AccountAddress,
427        event_handle: str,
428        field_name: str,
429        limit: Optional[int] = None,
430        start: Optional[int] = None,
431    ) -> List[dict]:
432        """
433        Retrieve events corresponding to an account address, event handle (struct name) and field name.
434
435        :param account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
436        :param event_handle: Name of struct to lookup event handle e.g., '0x1::account::Account'.
437        :param field_name: Name of field to lookup event handle e.g., 'withdraw_events'
438        :param limit: Max number of events to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
439        :param start: Starting sequence number of events.If unspecified, by default will retrieve the most recent.
440        :returns: Events corresponding to the provided account address, event handle and field name.
441        """
442        response = await self._get(
443            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/events/{event_handle}/{field_name}",
444            params={
445                "limit": limit,
446                "start": start,
447            },
448        )
449        if response.status_code >= 400:
450            raise ApiError(f"{response.text} - {account_address}", response.status_code)
451
452        return response.json()

Retrieve events corresponding to an account address, event handle (struct name) and field name.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of the account, with or without a '0x' prefix.
  • event_handle: Name of struct to lookup event handle e.g., '0x1::account:: Account'.
  • field_name: Name of field to lookup event handle e.g., 'withdraw_events'
  • limit: Max number of events to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
  • start: Starting sequence number of events.If unspecified, by default will retrieve the most recent. :returns: Events corresponding to the provided account address, event handle and field name.
async def current_timestamp(self) -> float: 
454    async def current_timestamp(self) -> float:
455        info = await self.info()
456        return float(info["ledger_timestamp"]) / 1_000_000
async def get_table_item( self, handle: str, key_type: str, value_type: str, key: Any, ledger_version: Optional[int] = None) -> Any: 
458    async def get_table_item(
459        self,
460        handle: str,
461        key_type: str,
462        value_type: str,
463        key: Any,
464        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
465    ) -> Any:
466        response = await self._post(
467            endpoint=f"tables/{handle}/item",
468            data={
469                "key_type": key_type,
470                "value_type": value_type,
471                "key": key,
472            },
473            params={"ledger_version": ledger_version},
474        )
475        if response.status_code >= 400:
476            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
477        return response.json()
async def aggregator_value( self, account_address: AccountAddress, resource_type: str, aggregator_path: List[str]) -> int: 
479    async def aggregator_value(
480        self,
481        account_address: AccountAddress,
482        resource_type: str,
483        aggregator_path: List[str],
484    ) -> int:
485        source = await self.account_resource(account_address, resource_type)
486        source_data = data = source["data"]
487
488        while len(aggregator_path) > 0:
489            key = aggregator_path.pop()
490            if key not in data:
491                raise ApiError(
492                    f"aggregator path not found in data: {source_data}", source_data
493                )
494            data = data[key]
495
496        if "vec" not in data:
497            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
498        data = data["vec"]
499        if len(data) != 1:
500            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
501        data = data[0]
502        if "aggregator" not in data:
503            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
504        data = data["aggregator"]
505        if "vec" not in data:
506            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
507        data = data["vec"]
508        if len(data) != 1:
509            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
510        data = data[0]
511        if "handle" not in data:
512            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
513        if "key" not in data:
514            raise ApiError(f"aggregator not found in data: {source_data}", source_data)
515        handle = data["handle"]
516        key = data["key"]
517        return int(await self.get_table_item(handle, "address", "u128", key))
async def info(self) -> Dict[str, str]: 
523    async def info(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
524        response = await self.client.get(self.base_url)
525        if response.status_code >= 400:
526            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
527        return response.json()
async def simulate_bcs_transaction( self, signed_transaction: SignedTransaction, estimate_gas_usage: bool = False) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
533    async def simulate_bcs_transaction(
534        self,
535        signed_transaction: SignedTransaction,
536        estimate_gas_usage: bool = False,
537    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
538        headers = {"Content-Type": "application/x.aptos.signed_transaction+bcs"}
539        params = {}
540        if estimate_gas_usage:
541            params = {
542                "estimate_gas_unit_price": "true",
543                "estimate_max_gas_amount": "true",
544            }
545
546        response = await self.client.post(
547            f"{self.base_url}/transactions/simulate",
548            params=params,
549            headers=headers,
550            content=signed_transaction.bytes(),
551        )
552        if response.status_code >= 400:
553            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
554
555        return response.json()
async def simulate_transaction( self, transaction: RawTransaction, sender: Account, estimate_gas_usage: bool = False) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
557    async def simulate_transaction(
558        self,
559        transaction: RawTransaction,
560        sender: Account,
561        estimate_gas_usage: bool = False,
562    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
563        # Note that simulated transactions are not signed and have all 0 signatures!
564        authenticator = sender.sign_simulated_transaction(transaction)
565        return await self.simulate_bcs_transaction(
566            signed_transaction=SignedTransaction(transaction, authenticator),
567            estimate_gas_usage=estimate_gas_usage,
568        )
async def submit_bcs_transaction( self, signed_transaction: SignedTransaction) -> str: 
570    async def submit_bcs_transaction(
571        self, signed_transaction: SignedTransaction
572    ) -> str:
573        headers = {"Content-Type": "application/x.aptos.signed_transaction+bcs"}
574        response = await self.client.post(
575            f"{self.base_url}/transactions",
576            headers=headers,
577            content=signed_transaction.bytes(),
578        )
579        if response.status_code >= 400:
580            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
581        return response.json()["hash"]
async def submit_and_wait_for_bcs_transaction( self, signed_transaction: SignedTransaction) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
583    async def submit_and_wait_for_bcs_transaction(
584        self, signed_transaction: SignedTransaction
585    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
586        txn_hash = await self.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
587        await self.wait_for_transaction(txn_hash)
588        return await self.transaction_by_hash(txn_hash)
async def transaction_pending(self, txn_hash: str) -> bool: 
590    async def transaction_pending(self, txn_hash: str) -> bool:
591        response = await self._get(endpoint=f"transactions/by_hash/{txn_hash}")
592        # TODO(@davidiw): consider raising a different error here, since this is an ambiguous state
593        if response.status_code == 404:
594            return True
595        if response.status_code >= 400:
596            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
597        return response.json()["type"] == "pending_transaction"
async def wait_for_transaction(self, txn_hash: str) -> None: 
599    async def wait_for_transaction(self, txn_hash: str) -> None:
600        """
601        Wait for a transaction to be confirmed on chain.
602
603        Waits up to the duration specified in client_config for a transaction
604        to move past pending state.
605
606        Args:
607            txn_hash: The transaction hash to wait for.
608
609        Raises:
610            TransactionTimeout: If the transaction is not confirmed within the timeout.
611            TransactionFailed: If the transaction was executed but failed.
612        """
613        from .errors import TransactionFailed, TransactionTimeout
614
615        count = 0
616        while await self.transaction_pending(txn_hash):
617            if count >= self.client_config.transaction_wait_in_seconds:
618                raise TransactionTimeout(
619                    txn_hash, self.client_config.transaction_wait_in_seconds
620                )
621            await asyncio.sleep(1)
622            count += 1
623
624        response = await self._get(endpoint=f"transactions/by_hash/{txn_hash}")
625        result = response.json()
626        if "success" not in result or not result["success"]:
627            vm_status = result.get("vm_status", "unknown")
628            raise TransactionFailed(txn_hash, vm_status)

Wait for a transaction to be confirmed on chain.

Waits up to the duration specified in client_config for a transaction to move past pending state.

Args: txn_hash: The transaction hash to wait for.

Raises: TransactionTimeout: If the transaction is not confirmed within the timeout. TransactionFailed: If the transaction was executed but failed.

async def account_transaction_sequence_number_status( self, address: AccountAddress, sequence_number: int) -> bool: 
630    async def account_transaction_sequence_number_status(
631        self, address: AccountAddress, sequence_number: int
632    ) -> bool:
633        """Retrieve the state of a transaction by account and sequence number."""
634        response = await self._get(
635            endpoint=f"accounts/{address}/transactions",
636            params={
637                "limit": 1,
638                "start": sequence_number,
639            },
640        )
641        if response.status_code >= 400:
642            logging.info(f"k {response}")
643            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
644        data = response.json()
645        return len(data) == 1 and data[0]["type"] != "pending_transaction"

Retrieve the state of a transaction by account and sequence number.

async def transaction_by_hash(self, txn_hash: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
647    async def transaction_by_hash(self, txn_hash: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
648        response = await self._get(endpoint=f"transactions/by_hash/{txn_hash}")
649        if response.status_code >= 400:
650            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
651        return response.json()
async def transaction_by_version(self, version: int) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
653    async def transaction_by_version(self, version: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
654        response = await self._get(endpoint=f"transactions/by_version/{version}")
655        if response.status_code >= 400:
656            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
657        return response.json()
async def transactions_by_account( self, account_address: AccountAddress, limit: Optional[int] = None, start: Optional[int] = None) -> List[dict]: 
659    async def transactions_by_account(
660        self,
661        account_address: AccountAddress,
662        limit: Optional[int] = None,
663        start: Optional[int] = None,
664    ) -> List[dict]:
665        """
666        Retrieves on-chain committed transactions from an account.
667
668        If the start version is too far in the past, a 410 will be returned. If no start version is given, it will
669        start at version 0.
670
671        To retrieve a pending transaction, use /transactions/by_hash.
672
673        :param account_address: Address of account with or without a 0x prefix.
674        :param limit: Max number of transactions to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
675        :param start: Account sequence number to start list of transactions. Defaults to latest transactions.
676        :returns: List of on-chain committed transactions from the specified account.
677        """
678        response = await self._get(
679            endpoint=f"accounts/{account_address}/transactions",
680            params={
681                "limit": limit,
682                "start": start,
683            },
684        )
685        if response.status_code >= 400:
686            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
687
688        return response.json()

Retrieves on-chain committed transactions from an account.

If the start version is too far in the past, a 410 will be returned. If no start version is given, it will start at version 0.

To retrieve a pending transaction, use /transactions/by_hash.

Parameters
  • account_address: Address of account with or without a 0x prefix.
  • limit: Max number of transactions to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
  • start: Account sequence number to start list of transactions. Defaults to latest transactions. :returns: List of on-chain committed transactions from the specified account.
async def transactions( self, limit: Optional[int] = None, start: Optional[int] = None) -> List[dict]: 
690    async def transactions(
691        self,
692        limit: Optional[int] = None,
693        start: Optional[int] = None,
694    ) -> List[dict]:
695        """
696        Retrieve on-chain committed transactions.
697
698        The page size and start ledger version can be provided to get a specific sequence of transactions. If the
699        version has been pruned, then a 410 will be returned. To retrieve a pending transaction,
700        use /transactions/by_hash.
701
702        :param limit: Max number of transactions to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
703        :param start: Ledger version to start list of transactions. Defaults to showing the latest transactions.
704        """
705        response = await self._get(
706            endpoint="transactions",
707            params={
708                "limit": limit,
709                "start": start,
710            },
711        )
712        if response.status_code >= 400:
713            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
714
715        return response.json()

Retrieve on-chain committed transactions.

The page size and start ledger version can be provided to get a specific sequence of transactions. If the version has been pruned, then a 410 will be returned. To retrieve a pending transaction, use /transactions/by_hash.

Parameters
  • limit: Max number of transactions to retrieve. If not provided, defaults to default page size.
  • start: Ledger version to start list of transactions. Defaults to showing the latest transactions.
async def create_multi_agent_bcs_transaction( self, sender: Account, secondary_accounts: List[Account], payload: TransactionPayload) -> SignedTransaction: 
721    async def create_multi_agent_bcs_transaction(
722        self,
723        sender: Account,
724        secondary_accounts: List[Account],
725        payload: TransactionPayload,
726    ) -> SignedTransaction:
727        raw_transaction = MultiAgentRawTransaction(
728            RawTransaction(
729                sender.address(),
730                await self.account_sequence_number(sender.address()),
731                payload,
732                self.client_config.max_gas_amount,
733                self.client_config.gas_unit_price,
734                int(time.time()) + self.client_config.expiration_ttl,
735                await self.chain_id(),
736            ),
737            [x.address() for x in secondary_accounts],
738        )
739
740        authenticator = Authenticator(
741            MultiAgentAuthenticator(
742                sender.sign_transaction(raw_transaction),
743                [
744                    (
745                        x.address(),
746                        x.sign_transaction(raw_transaction),
747                    )
748                    for x in secondary_accounts
749                ],
750            )
751        )
752
753        return SignedTransaction(raw_transaction.inner(), authenticator)
async def create_bcs_transaction( self, sender: Account | AccountAddress, payload: TransactionPayload, sequence_number: Optional[int] = None) -> RawTransaction: 
755    async def create_bcs_transaction(
756        self,
757        sender: Account | AccountAddress,
758        payload: TransactionPayload,
759        sequence_number: Optional[int] = None,
760    ) -> RawTransaction:
761        if isinstance(sender, Account):
762            sender_address = sender.address()
763        else:
764            sender_address = sender
765
766        sequence_number = (
767            sequence_number
768            if sequence_number is not None
769            else await self.account_sequence_number(sender_address)
770        )
771        return RawTransaction(
772            sender_address,
773            sequence_number,
774            payload,
775            self.client_config.max_gas_amount,
776            self.client_config.gas_unit_price,
777            int(time.time()) + self.client_config.expiration_ttl,
778            await self.chain_id(),
779        )
async def create_bcs_signed_transaction( self, sender: Account, payload: TransactionPayload, sequence_number: Optional[int] = None) -> SignedTransaction: 
781    async def create_bcs_signed_transaction(
782        self,
783        sender: Account,
784        payload: TransactionPayload,
785        sequence_number: Optional[int] = None,
786    ) -> SignedTransaction:
787        raw_transaction = await self.create_bcs_transaction(
788            sender, payload, sequence_number
789        )
790        authenticator = sender.sign_transaction(raw_transaction)
791        return SignedTransaction(raw_transaction, authenticator)
async def bcs_transfer( self, sender: Account, recipient: AccountAddress, amount: int, sequence_number: Optional[int] = None) -> str: 
798    async def bcs_transfer(
799        self,
800        sender: Account,
801        recipient: AccountAddress,
802        amount: int,
803        sequence_number: Optional[int] = None,
804    ) -> str:
805        transaction_arguments = [
806            TransactionArgument(recipient, Serializer.struct),
807            TransactionArgument(amount, Serializer.u64),
808        ]
809
810        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
811            "0x1::aptos_account",
812            "transfer",
813            [],
814            transaction_arguments,
815        )
816
817        signed_transaction = await self.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
818            sender, TransactionPayload(payload), sequence_number=sequence_number
819        )
820        return await self.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)  # <:!:bcs_transfer
async def transfer_coins( self, sender: Account, recipient: AccountAddress, coin_type: str, amount: int, sequence_number: Optional[int] = None) -> str: 
822    async def transfer_coins(
823        self,
824        sender: Account,
825        recipient: AccountAddress,
826        coin_type: str,
827        amount: int,
828        sequence_number: Optional[int] = None,
829    ) -> str:
830        transaction_arguments = [
831            TransactionArgument(recipient, Serializer.struct),
832            TransactionArgument(amount, Serializer.u64),
833        ]
834
835        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
836            "0x1::aptos_account",
837            "transfer_coins",
838            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str(coin_type))],
839            transaction_arguments,
840        )
841
842        signed_transaction = await self.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
843            sender, TransactionPayload(payload), sequence_number=sequence_number
844        )
845        return await self.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def transfer_object( self, owner: Account, object: AccountAddress, to: AccountAddress) -> str: 
847    async def transfer_object(
848        self, owner: Account, object: AccountAddress, to: AccountAddress
849    ) -> str:
850        transaction_arguments = [
851            TransactionArgument(object, Serializer.struct),
852            TransactionArgument(to, Serializer.struct),
853        ]
854
855        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
856            "0x1::object",
857            "transfer_call",
858            [],
859            transaction_arguments,
860        )
861
862        signed_transaction = await self.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
863            owner,
864            TransactionPayload(payload),
865        )
866        return await self.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def view( self, function: str, type_arguments: List[str], arguments: List[str], ledger_version: Optional[int] = None) -> bytes: 
868    async def view(
869        self,
870        function: str,
871        type_arguments: List[str],
872        arguments: List[str],
873        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
874    ) -> bytes:
875        """
876        Execute a view Move function with the given parameters and return its execution result.
877
878        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
879        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
880
881        :param function: Entry function id is string representation of an entry function defined on-chain.
882        :param type_arguments: Type arguments of the function.
883        :param arguments: Arguments of the function.
884        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
885        :returns: Execution result.
886        """
887        response = await self._post(
888            endpoint="view",
889            params={
890                "ledger_version": ledger_version,
891            },
892            headers={
893                "Accept": "application/json",
894                "Content-Type": "application/json",
895            },
896            data={
897                "function": function,
898                "type_arguments": type_arguments,
899                "arguments": arguments,
900            },
901        )
902        if response.status_code >= 400:
903            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
904
905        return response.content

Execute a view Move function with the given parameters and return its execution result.

The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.

Parameters
  • function: Entry function id is string representation of an entry function defined on-chain.
  • type_arguments: Type arguments of the function.
  • arguments: Arguments of the function.
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version. :returns: Execution result.
async def view_bcs_payload( self, module: str, function: str, ty_args: List[TypeTag], args: List[TransactionArgument], ledger_version: Optional[int] = None) -> Any: 
907    async def view_bcs_payload(
908        self,
909        module: str,
910        function: str,
911        ty_args: List[TypeTag],
912        args: List[TransactionArgument],
913        ledger_version: Optional[int] = None,
914    ) -> Any:
915        """
916        Execute a view Move function with the given parameters and return its execution result.
917        Note, this differs from `view` as in this expects bcs compatible inputs and submits the
918        view function in bcs format. This is convenient for clients that execute functions in
919        transactions similar to view functions.
920
921        The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version
922        has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.
923
924        :param function: Entry function id is string representation of an entry function defined on-chain.
