925        :param type_arguments: Type arguments of the function.
926        :param arguments: Arguments of the function.
927        :param ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version.
928        :returns: Execution result.
929        """
930        request = f"{self.base_url}/view"
931        if ledger_version:
932            request = f"{request}?ledger_version={ledger_version}"
933
934        view_data = EntryFunction.natural(module, function, ty_args, args)
935        ser = Serializer()
936        view_data.serialize(ser)
937        headers = {"Content-Type": "application/x.aptos.view_function+bcs"}
938        response = await self.client.post(
939            request, headers=headers, content=ser.output()
940        )
941        if response.status_code >= 400:
942            raise ApiError(response.text, response.status_code)
943        return response.json()

Execute a view Move function with the given parameters and return its execution result. Note, this differs from view as in this expects bcs compatible inputs and submits the view function in bcs format. This is convenient for clients that execute functions in transactions similar to view functions.

The Aptos nodes prune account state history, via a configurable time window. If the requested ledger version has been pruned, the server responds with a 410.

Parameters
  • function: Entry function id is string representation of an entry function defined on-chain.
  • type_arguments: Type arguments of the function.
  • arguments: Arguments of the function.
  • ledger_version: Ledger version to get state of account. If not provided, it will be the latest version. :returns: Execution result.
class AptosTokenClient: 
360class AptosTokenClient:
361    """A wrapper around reading and mutating Digital Assets also known as Token Objects"""
362
363    client: RestClient
364
365    def __init__(self, client: RestClient):
366        self.client = client
367
368    async def read_object(self, address: AccountAddress) -> ReadObject:
369        resources = {}
370
371        read_resources = await self.client.account_resources(address)
372        for resource in read_resources:
373            if resource["type"] in ReadObject.resource_map:
374                resource_obj = ReadObject.resource_map[resource["type"]]
375                resources[resource_obj] = resource_obj.parse(resource["data"])
376        return ReadObject(resources)
377
378    @staticmethod
379    def create_collection_payload(
380        description: str,
381        max_supply: int,
382        name: str,
383        uri: str,
384        mutable_description: bool,
385        mutable_royalty: bool,
386        mutable_uri: bool,
387        mutable_token_description: bool,
388        mutable_token_name: bool,
389        mutable_token_properties: bool,
390        mutable_token_uri: bool,
391        tokens_burnable_by_creator: bool,
392        tokens_freezable_by_creator: bool,
393        royalty_numerator: int,
394        royalty_denominator: int,
395    ) -> TransactionPayload:
396        transaction_arguments = [
397            TransactionArgument(description, Serializer.str),
398            TransactionArgument(max_supply, Serializer.u64),
399            TransactionArgument(name, Serializer.str),
400            TransactionArgument(uri, Serializer.str),
401            TransactionArgument(mutable_description, Serializer.bool),
402            TransactionArgument(mutable_royalty, Serializer.bool),
403            TransactionArgument(mutable_uri, Serializer.bool),
404            TransactionArgument(mutable_token_description, Serializer.bool),
405            TransactionArgument(mutable_token_name, Serializer.bool),
406            TransactionArgument(mutable_token_properties, Serializer.bool),
407            TransactionArgument(mutable_token_uri, Serializer.bool),
408            TransactionArgument(tokens_burnable_by_creator, Serializer.bool),
409            TransactionArgument(tokens_freezable_by_creator, Serializer.bool),
410            TransactionArgument(royalty_numerator, Serializer.u64),
411            TransactionArgument(royalty_denominator, Serializer.u64),
412        ]
413
414        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
415            "0x4::aptos_token",
416            "create_collection",
417            [],
418            transaction_arguments,
419        )
420
421        return TransactionPayload(payload)
422
423    # :!:>create_collection
424    async def create_collection(
425        self,
426        creator: Account,
427        description: str,
428        max_supply: int,
429        name: str,
430        uri: str,
431        mutable_description: bool,
432        mutable_royalty: bool,
433        mutable_uri: bool,
434        mutable_token_description: bool,
435        mutable_token_name: bool,
436        mutable_token_properties: bool,
437        mutable_token_uri: bool,
438        tokens_burnable_by_creator: bool,
439        tokens_freezable_by_creator: bool,
440        royalty_numerator: int,
441        royalty_denominator: int,
442    ) -> str:  # <:!:create_collection
443        payload = AptosTokenClient.create_collection_payload(
444            description,
445            max_supply,
446            name,
447            uri,
448            mutable_description,
449            mutable_royalty,
450            mutable_uri,
451            mutable_token_description,
452            mutable_token_name,
453            mutable_token_properties,
454            mutable_token_uri,
455            tokens_burnable_by_creator,
456            tokens_freezable_by_creator,
457            royalty_numerator,
458            royalty_denominator,
459        )
460        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
461            creator, payload
462        )
463        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
464
465    @staticmethod
466    def mint_token_payload(
467        collection: str,
468        description: str,
469        name: str,
470        uri: str,
471        properties: PropertyMap,
472    ) -> TransactionPayload:
473        (property_names, property_types, property_values) = properties.to_tuple()
474        transaction_arguments = [
475            TransactionArgument(collection, Serializer.str),
476            TransactionArgument(description, Serializer.str),
477            TransactionArgument(name, Serializer.str),
478            TransactionArgument(uri, Serializer.str),
479            TransactionArgument(
480                property_names, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.str)
481            ),
482            TransactionArgument(
483                property_types, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.str)
484            ),
485            TransactionArgument(
486                property_values, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
487            ),
488        ]
489
490        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
491            "0x4::aptos_token",
492            "mint",
493            [],
494            transaction_arguments,
495        )
496
497        return TransactionPayload(payload)
498
499    # :!:>mint_token
500    async def mint_token(
501        self,
502        creator: Account,
503        collection: str,
504        description: str,
505        name: str,
506        uri: str,
507        properties: PropertyMap,
508    ) -> str:  # <:!:mint_token
509        payload = AptosTokenClient.mint_token_payload(
510            collection, description, name, uri, properties
511        )
512        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
513            creator, payload
514        )
515        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
516
517    async def mint_soul_bound_token(
518        self,
519        creator: Account,
520        collection: str,
521        description: str,
522        name: str,
523        uri: str,
524        properties: PropertyMap,
525        soul_bound_to: AccountAddress,
526    ):
527        (property_names, property_types, property_values) = properties.to_tuple()
528        transaction_arguments = [
529            TransactionArgument(collection, Serializer.str),
530            TransactionArgument(description, Serializer.str),
531            TransactionArgument(name, Serializer.str),
532            TransactionArgument(uri, Serializer.str),
533            TransactionArgument(
534                property_names, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.str)
535            ),
536            TransactionArgument(
537                property_types, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.str)
538            ),
539            TransactionArgument(
540                property_values, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
541            ),
542            TransactionArgument(soul_bound_to, Serializer.struct),
543        ]
544
545        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
546            "0x4::aptos_token",
547            "mint_soul_bound",
548            [],
549            transaction_arguments,
550        )
551
552        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
553            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
554        )
555        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
556
557    # :!:>transfer_token
558    async def transfer_token(
559        self, owner: Account, token: AccountAddress, to: AccountAddress
560    ) -> str:
561        return await self.client.transfer_object(owner, token, to)  # <:!:transfer_token
562
563    async def burn_token(self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str:
564        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
565            "0x4::aptos_token",
566            "burn",
567            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
568            [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)],
569        )
570
571        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
572            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
573        )
574        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
575
576    async def freeze_token(self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str:
577        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
578            "0x4::aptos_token",
579            "freeze_transfer",
580            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
581            [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)],
582        )
583
584        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
585            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
586        )
587        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
588
589    async def unfreeze_token(self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str:
590        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
591            "0x4::aptos_token",
592            "unfreeze_transfer",
593            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
594            [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)],
595        )
596
597        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
598            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
599        )
600        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
601
602    async def add_token_property(
603        self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, prop: Property
604    ) -> str:
605        transaction_arguments = [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)]
606        transaction_arguments.extend(prop.to_transaction_arguments())
607
608        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
609            "0x4::aptos_token",
610            "add_property",
611            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
612            transaction_arguments,
613        )
614
615        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
616            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
617        )
618        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
619
620    async def remove_token_property(
621        self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, name: str
622    ) -> str:
623        transaction_arguments = [
624            TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct),
625            TransactionArgument(name, Serializer.str),
626        ]
627
628        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
629            "0x4::aptos_token",
630            "remove_property",
631            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
632            transaction_arguments,
633        )
634
635        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
636            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
637        )
638        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
639
640    async def update_token_property(
641        self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, prop: Property
642    ) -> str:
643        transaction_arguments = [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)]
644        transaction_arguments.extend(prop.to_transaction_arguments())
645
646        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
647            "0x4::aptos_token",
648            "update_property",
649            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
650            transaction_arguments,
651        )
652
653        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
654            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
655        )
656        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
657
658    async def tokens_minted_from_transaction(
659        self, txn_hash: str
660    ) -> List[AccountAddress]:
661        output = await self.client.transaction_by_hash(txn_hash)
662        mints = []
663        for event in output["events"]:
664            if event["type"] not in (
665                "0x4::collection::MintEvent",
666                "0x4::collection::Mint",
667            ):
668                continue
669            mints.append(AccountAddress.from_str_relaxed(event["data"]["token"]))
670        return mints

A wrapper around reading and mutating Digital Assets also known as Token Objects

AptosTokenClient(client: RestClient) 
365    def __init__(self, client: RestClient):
366        self.client = client
client: RestClient
async def read_object( self, address: AccountAddress) -> ReadObject: 
368    async def read_object(self, address: AccountAddress) -> ReadObject:
369        resources = {}
370
371        read_resources = await self.client.account_resources(address)
372        for resource in read_resources:
373            if resource["type"] in ReadObject.resource_map:
374                resource_obj = ReadObject.resource_map[resource["type"]]
375                resources[resource_obj] = resource_obj.parse(resource["data"])
376        return ReadObject(resources)
@staticmethod
def create_collection_payload( description: str, max_supply: int, name: str, uri: str, mutable_description: bool, mutable_royalty: bool, mutable_uri: bool, mutable_token_description: bool, mutable_token_name: bool, mutable_token_properties: bool, mutable_token_uri: bool, tokens_burnable_by_creator: bool, tokens_freezable_by_creator: bool, royalty_numerator: int, royalty_denominator: int) -> TransactionPayload: 
378    @staticmethod
379    def create_collection_payload(
380        description: str,
381        max_supply: int,
382        name: str,
383        uri: str,
384        mutable_description: bool,
385        mutable_royalty: bool,
386        mutable_uri: bool,
387        mutable_token_description: bool,
388        mutable_token_name: bool,
389        mutable_token_properties: bool,
390        mutable_token_uri: bool,
391        tokens_burnable_by_creator: bool,
392        tokens_freezable_by_creator: bool,
393        royalty_numerator: int,
394        royalty_denominator: int,
395    ) -> TransactionPayload:
396        transaction_arguments = [
397            TransactionArgument(description, Serializer.str),
398            TransactionArgument(max_supply, Serializer.u64),
399            TransactionArgument(name, Serializer.str),
400            TransactionArgument(uri, Serializer.str),
401            TransactionArgument(mutable_description, Serializer.bool),
402            TransactionArgument(mutable_royalty, Serializer.bool),
403            TransactionArgument(mutable_uri, Serializer.bool),
404            TransactionArgument(mutable_token_description, Serializer.bool),
405            TransactionArgument(mutable_token_name, Serializer.bool),
406            TransactionArgument(mutable_token_properties, Serializer.bool),
407            TransactionArgument(mutable_token_uri, Serializer.bool),
408            TransactionArgument(tokens_burnable_by_creator, Serializer.bool),
409            TransactionArgument(tokens_freezable_by_creator, Serializer.bool),
410            TransactionArgument(royalty_numerator, Serializer.u64),
411            TransactionArgument(royalty_denominator, Serializer.u64),
412        ]
413
414        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
415            "0x4::aptos_token",
416            "create_collection",
417            [],
418            transaction_arguments,
419        )
420
421        return TransactionPayload(payload)
async def create_collection( self, creator: Account, description: str, max_supply: int, name: str, uri: str, mutable_description: bool, mutable_royalty: bool, mutable_uri: bool, mutable_token_description: bool, mutable_token_name: bool, mutable_token_properties: bool, mutable_token_uri: bool, tokens_burnable_by_creator: bool, tokens_freezable_by_creator: bool, royalty_numerator: int, royalty_denominator: int) -> str: 
424    async def create_collection(
425        self,
426        creator: Account,
427        description: str,
428        max_supply: int,
429        name: str,
430        uri: str,
431        mutable_description: bool,
432        mutable_royalty: bool,
433        mutable_uri: bool,
434        mutable_token_description: bool,
435        mutable_token_name: bool,
436        mutable_token_properties: bool,
437        mutable_token_uri: bool,
438        tokens_burnable_by_creator: bool,
439        tokens_freezable_by_creator: bool,
440        royalty_numerator: int,
441        royalty_denominator: int,
442    ) -> str:  # <:!:create_collection
443        payload = AptosTokenClient.create_collection_payload(
444            description,
445            max_supply,
446            name,
447            uri,
448            mutable_description,
449            mutable_royalty,
450            mutable_uri,
451            mutable_token_description,
452            mutable_token_name,
453            mutable_token_properties,
454            mutable_token_uri,
455            tokens_burnable_by_creator,
456            tokens_freezable_by_creator,
457            royalty_numerator,
458            royalty_denominator,
459        )
460        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
461            creator, payload
462        )
463        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
@staticmethod
def mint_token_payload( collection: str, description: str, name: str, uri: str, properties: PropertyMap) -> TransactionPayload: 
465    @staticmethod
466    def mint_token_payload(
467        collection: str,
468        description: str,
469        name: str,
470        uri: str,
471        properties: PropertyMap,
472    ) -> TransactionPayload:
473        (property_names, property_types, property_values) = properties.to_tuple()
474        transaction_arguments = [
475            TransactionArgument(collection, Serializer.str),
476            TransactionArgument(description, Serializer.str),
477            TransactionArgument(name, Serializer.str),
478            TransactionArgument(uri, Serializer.str),
479            TransactionArgument(
480                property_names, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.str)
481            ),
482            TransactionArgument(
483                property_types, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.str)
484            ),
485            TransactionArgument(
486                property_values, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
487            ),
488        ]
489
490        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
491            "0x4::aptos_token",
492            "mint",
493            [],
494            transaction_arguments,
495        )
496
497        return TransactionPayload(payload)
async def mint_token( self, creator: Account, collection: str, description: str, name: str, uri: str, properties: PropertyMap) -> str: 
500    async def mint_token(
501        self,
502        creator: Account,
503        collection: str,
504        description: str,
505        name: str,
506        uri: str,
507        properties: PropertyMap,
508    ) -> str:  # <:!:mint_token
509        payload = AptosTokenClient.mint_token_payload(
510            collection, description, name, uri, properties
511        )
512        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
513            creator, payload
514        )
515        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def mint_soul_bound_token( self, creator: Account, collection: str, description: str, name: str, uri: str, properties: PropertyMap, soul_bound_to: AccountAddress): 
517    async def mint_soul_bound_token(
518        self,
519        creator: Account,
520        collection: str,
521        description: str,
522        name: str,
523        uri: str,
524        properties: PropertyMap,
525        soul_bound_to: AccountAddress,
526    ):
527        (property_names, property_types, property_values) = properties.to_tuple()
528        transaction_arguments = [
529            TransactionArgument(collection, Serializer.str),
530            TransactionArgument(description, Serializer.str),
531            TransactionArgument(name, Serializer.str),
532            TransactionArgument(uri, Serializer.str),
533            TransactionArgument(
534                property_names, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.str)
535            ),
536            TransactionArgument(
537                property_types, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.str)
538            ),
539            TransactionArgument(
540                property_values, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
541            ),
542            TransactionArgument(soul_bound_to, Serializer.struct),
543        ]
544
545        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
546            "0x4::aptos_token",
547            "mint_soul_bound",
548            [],
549            transaction_arguments,
550        )
551
552        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
553            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
554        )
555        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def transfer_token( self, owner: Account, token: AccountAddress, to: AccountAddress) -> str: 
558    async def transfer_token(
559        self, owner: Account, token: AccountAddress, to: AccountAddress
560    ) -> str:
561        return await self.client.transfer_object(owner, token, to)  # <:!:transfer_token
async def burn_token( self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str: 
563    async def burn_token(self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str:
564        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
565            "0x4::aptos_token",
566            "burn",
567            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
568            [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)],
569        )
570
571        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
572            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
573        )
574        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def freeze_token( self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str: 
576    async def freeze_token(self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str:
577        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
578            "0x4::aptos_token",
579            "freeze_transfer",
580            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
581            [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)],
582        )
583
584        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
585            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
586        )
587        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def unfreeze_token( self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str: 
589    async def unfreeze_token(self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress) -> str:
590        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
591            "0x4::aptos_token",
592            "unfreeze_transfer",
593            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
594            [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)],
595        )
596
597        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
598            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
599        )
600        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def add_token_property( self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, prop: Property) -> str: 
602    async def add_token_property(
603        self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, prop: Property
604    ) -> str:
605        transaction_arguments = [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)]
606        transaction_arguments.extend(prop.to_transaction_arguments())
607
608        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
609            "0x4::aptos_token",
610            "add_property",
611            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
612            transaction_arguments,
613        )
614
615        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
616            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
617        )
618        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def remove_token_property( self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, name: str) -> str: 
620    async def remove_token_property(
621        self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, name: str
622    ) -> str:
623        transaction_arguments = [
624            TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct),
625            TransactionArgument(name, Serializer.str),
626        ]
627
628        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
629            "0x4::aptos_token",
630            "remove_property",
631            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
632            transaction_arguments,
633        )
634
635        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
636            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
637        )
638        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def update_token_property( self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, prop: Property) -> str: 
640    async def update_token_property(
641        self, creator: Account, token: AccountAddress, prop: Property
642    ) -> str:
643        transaction_arguments = [TransactionArgument(token, Serializer.struct)]
644        transaction_arguments.extend(prop.to_transaction_arguments())
645
646        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
647            "0x4::aptos_token",
648            "update_property",
649            [TypeTag(StructTag.from_str("0x4::token::Token"))],
650            transaction_arguments,
651        )
652
653        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
654            creator, TransactionPayload(payload)
655        )
656        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def tokens_minted_from_transaction(self, txn_hash: str) -> List[AccountAddress]: 
658    async def tokens_minted_from_transaction(
659        self, txn_hash: str
660    ) -> List[AccountAddress]:
661        output = await self.client.transaction_by_hash(txn_hash)
662        mints = []
663        for event in output["events"]:
664            if event["type"] not in (
665                "0x4::collection::MintEvent",
666                "0x4::collection::Mint",
667            ):
668                continue
669            mints.append(AccountAddress.from_str_relaxed(event["data"]["token"]))
670        return mints
@dataclass
class Object: 
25@dataclass
26class Object:
27    """
28    Represents an Aptos object (ObjectCore resource).
29
30    Objects are the foundation for Digital Assets on Aptos. Every token,
31    collection, and other on-chain asset is an Object.
32
33    Attributes:
34        allow_ungated_transfer: Whether the object can be transferred freely.
35        owner: The current owner's address.
36    """
37
38    allow_ungated_transfer: bool
39    owner: AccountAddress
40    struct_tag: ClassVar[str] = "0x1::object::ObjectCore"
41
42    @staticmethod
43    def parse(resource: dict[str, Any]) -> Object:
44        """Parse an Object from a JSON resource."""
45        return Object(
46            resource["allow_ungated_transfer"],
47            AccountAddress.from_str_relaxed(resource["owner"]),
48        )
49
50    def __str__(self) -> str:
51        return f"Object[allow_ungated_transfer: {self.allow_ungated_transfer}, owner: {self.owner}]"

Represents an Aptos object (ObjectCore resource).

Objects are the foundation for Digital Assets on Aptos. Every token, collection, and other on-chain asset is an Object.

Attributes: allow_ungated_transfer: Whether the object can be transferred freely. owner: The current owner's address.

Object( allow_ungated_transfer: bool, owner: AccountAddress)
allow_ungated_transfer: bool
owner: AccountAddress
struct_tag: ClassVar[str] = '0x1::object::ObjectCore'
@staticmethod
def parse(resource: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> Object: 
42    @staticmethod
43    def parse(resource: dict[str, Any]) -> Object:
44        """Parse an Object from a JSON resource."""
45        return Object(
46            resource["allow_ungated_transfer"],
47            AccountAddress.from_str_relaxed(resource["owner"]),
48        )

Parse an Object from a JSON resource.

class Property: 
173class Property:
174    name: str
175    property_type: str
176    value: Any
177
178    BOOL: int = 0
179    U8: int = 1
180    U16: int = 2
181    U32: int = 3
182    U64: int = 4
183    U128: int = 5
184    U256: int = 6
185    ADDRESS: int = 7
186    BYTE_VECTOR: int = 8
187    STRING: int = 9
188
189    def __init__(self, name: str, property_type: str, value: Any):
190        self.name = name
191        self.property_type = property_type
192        self.value = value
193
194    def __str__(self) -> str:
195        return f"Property[{self.name}, {self.property_type}, {self.value}]"
196
197    def serialize_value(self) -> bytes:
198        ser = Serializer()
199        if self.property_type == "bool":
200            Serializer.bool(ser, self.value)
201        elif self.property_type == "u8":
202            Serializer.u8(ser, self.value)
203        elif self.property_type == "u16":
204            Serializer.u16(ser, self.value)
205        elif self.property_type == "u32":
206            Serializer.u32(ser, self.value)
207        elif self.property_type == "u64":
208            Serializer.u64(ser, self.value)
209        elif self.property_type == "u128":
210            Serializer.u128(ser, self.value)
211        elif self.property_type == "u256":
212            Serializer.u256(ser, self.value)
213        elif self.property_type == "address":
214            Serializer.struct(ser, self.value)
215        elif self.property_type == "0x1::string::String":
216            Serializer.str(ser, self.value)
217        elif self.property_type == "vector<u8>":
218            Serializer.to_bytes(ser, self.value)
219        else:
220            raise InvalidPropertyType(self.property_type)
221        return ser.output()
222
223    def to_transaction_arguments(self) -> List[TransactionArgument]:
224        return [
225            TransactionArgument(self.name, Serializer.str),
226            TransactionArgument(self.property_type, Serializer.str),
227            TransactionArgument(self.serialize_value(), Serializer.to_bytes),
228        ]
229
230    @staticmethod
231    def parse(name: str, property_type: int, value: bytes) -> Property:
232        deserializer = Deserializer(value)
233
234        if property_type == Property.BOOL:
235            return Property(name, "bool", deserializer.bool())
236        elif property_type == Property.U8:
237            return Property(name, "u8", deserializer.u8())
238        elif property_type == Property.U16:
239            return Property(name, "u16", deserializer.u16())
240        elif property_type == Property.U32:
241            return Property(name, "u32", deserializer.u32())
242        elif property_type == Property.U64:
243            return Property(name, "u64", deserializer.u64())
244        elif property_type == Property.U128:
245            return Property(name, "u128", deserializer.u128())
246        elif property_type == Property.U256:
247            return Property(name, "u256", deserializer.u256())
248        elif property_type == Property.ADDRESS:
249            return Property(name, "address", AccountAddress.deserialize(deserializer))
250        elif property_type == Property.STRING:
251            return Property(name, "0x1::string::String", deserializer.str())
252        elif property_type == Property.BYTE_VECTOR:
253            return Property(name, "vector<u8>", deserializer.to_bytes())
254        raise InvalidPropertyType(property_type)
255
256    @staticmethod
257    def bool(name: str, value: bool) -> Property:
258        return Property(name, "bool", value)
259
260    @staticmethod
261    def u8(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
262        return Property(name, "u8", value)
263
264    @staticmethod
265    def u16(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
266        return Property(name, "u16", value)
267
268    @staticmethod
269    def u32(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
270        return Property(name, "u32", value)
271
272    @staticmethod
273    def u64(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
274        return Property(name, "u64", value)
275
276    @staticmethod
277    def u128(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
278        return Property(name, "u128", value)
279
280    @staticmethod
281    def u256(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
282        return Property(name, "u256", value)
283
284    @staticmethod
285    def string(name: str, value: str) -> Property:
286        return Property(name, "0x1::string::String", value)
287
288    @staticmethod
289    def bytes(name: str, value: bytes) -> Property:
290        return Property(name, "vector<u8>", value)
Property(name: str, property_type: str, value: Any) 
189    def __init__(self, name: str, property_type: str, value: Any):
190        self.name = name
191        self.property_type = property_type
192        self.value = value
name: str
property_type: str
value: Any
BOOL: int = 0
U8: int = 1
U16: int = 2
U32: int = 3
U64: int = 4
U128: int = 5
U256: int = 6
ADDRESS: int = 7
BYTE_VECTOR: int = 8
STRING: int = 9
def serialize_value(self) -> <staticmethod(<function Property.bytes at 0x10b75b920>)>: 
197    def serialize_value(self) -> bytes:
198        ser = Serializer()
199        if self.property_type == "bool":
200            Serializer.bool(ser, self.value)
201        elif self.property_type == "u8":
202            Serializer.u8(ser, self.value)
203        elif self.property_type == "u16":
204            Serializer.u16(ser, self.value)
205        elif self.property_type == "u32":
206            Serializer.u32(ser, self.value)
207        elif self.property_type == "u64":
208            Serializer.u64(ser, self.value)
209        elif self.property_type == "u128":
210            Serializer.u128(ser, self.value)
211        elif self.property_type == "u256":
212            Serializer.u256(ser, self.value)
213        elif self.property_type == "address":
214            Serializer.struct(ser, self.value)
215        elif self.property_type == "0x1::string::String":
216            Serializer.str(ser, self.value)
217        elif self.property_type == "vector<u8>":
218            Serializer.to_bytes(ser, self.value)
219        else:
220            raise InvalidPropertyType(self.property_type)
221        return ser.output()
def to_transaction_arguments(self) -> List[TransactionArgument]: 
223    def to_transaction_arguments(self) -> List[TransactionArgument]:
224        return [
225            TransactionArgument(self.name, Serializer.str),
226            TransactionArgument(self.property_type, Serializer.str),
227            TransactionArgument(self.serialize_value(), Serializer.to_bytes),
228        ]
@staticmethod
def parse( name: str, property_type: int, value: <staticmethod(<function Property.bytes>)>) -> Property: 
230    @staticmethod
231    def parse(name: str, property_type: int, value: bytes) -> Property:
232        deserializer = Deserializer(value)
233
234        if property_type == Property.BOOL:
235            return Property(name, "bool", deserializer.bool())
236        elif property_type == Property.U8:
237            return Property(name, "u8", deserializer.u8())
238        elif property_type == Property.U16:
239            return Property(name, "u16", deserializer.u16())
240        elif property_type == Property.U32:
241            return Property(name, "u32", deserializer.u32())
242        elif property_type == Property.U64:
243            return Property(name, "u64", deserializer.u64())
244        elif property_type == Property.U128:
245            return Property(name, "u128", deserializer.u128())
246        elif property_type == Property.U256:
247            return Property(name, "u256", deserializer.u256())
248        elif property_type == Property.ADDRESS:
249            return Property(name, "address", AccountAddress.deserialize(deserializer))
250        elif property_type == Property.STRING:
251            return Property(name, "0x1::string::String", deserializer.str())
252        elif property_type == Property.BYTE_VECTOR:
253            return Property(name, "vector<u8>", deserializer.to_bytes())
254        raise InvalidPropertyType(property_type)
@staticmethod
def bool( name: str, value: <staticmethod(<function Property.bool>)>) -> Property: 
256    @staticmethod
257    def bool(name: str, value: bool) -> Property:
258        return Property(name, "bool", value)
@staticmethod
def u8(name: str, value: int) -> Property: 
260    @staticmethod
261    def u8(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
262        return Property(name, "u8", value)
@staticmethod
def u16(name: str, value: int) -> Property: 
264    @staticmethod
265    def u16(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
266        return Property(name, "u16", value)
@staticmethod
def u32(name: str, value: int) -> Property: 
268    @staticmethod
269    def u32(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
270        return Property(name, "u32", value)
@staticmethod
def u64(name: str, value: int) -> Property: 
272    @staticmethod
273    def u64(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
274        return Property(name, "u64", value)
@staticmethod
def u128(name: str, value: int) -> Property: 
276    @staticmethod
277    def u128(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
278        return Property(name, "u128", value)
@staticmethod
def u256(name: str, value: int) -> Property: 
280    @staticmethod
281    def u256(name: str, value: int) -> Property:
282        return Property(name, "u256", value)
@staticmethod
def string(name: str, value: str) -> Property: 
284    @staticmethod
285    def string(name: str, value: str) -> Property:
286        return Property(name, "0x1::string::String", value)
@staticmethod
def bytes( name: str, value: <staticmethod(<function Property.bytes>)>) -> Property: 
288    @staticmethod
289    def bytes(name: str, value: bytes) -> Property:
290        return Property(name, "vector<u8>", value)
class PropertyMap: 
293class PropertyMap:
294    properties: List[Property]
295
296    struct_tag: str = "0x4::property_map::PropertyMap"
297
298    def __init__(self, properties: List[Property]):
299        self.properties = properties
300
301    def __str__(self) -> str:
302        response = "PropertyMap["
303        for prop in self.properties:
304            response += f"{prop}, "
305        if len(self.properties) > 0:
306            response = response[:-2]
307        response += "]"
308        return response
309
310    def to_tuple(self) -> Tuple[List[str], List[str], List[bytes]]:
311        names = []
312        types = []
313        values = []
314
315        for prop in self.properties:
316            names.append(prop.name)
317            types.append(prop.property_type)
318            values.append(prop.serialize_value())
319
320        return (names, types, values)
321
322    @staticmethod
323    def parse(resource: dict[str, Any]) -> PropertyMap:
324        props = resource["inner"]["data"]
325        properties = []
326        for prop in props:
327            properties.append(
328                Property.parse(
329                    prop["key"],
330                    prop["value"]["type"],
331                    bytes.fromhex(prop["value"]["value"][2:]),
332                )
333            )
334
335        return PropertyMap(properties)
PropertyMap(properties: List[Property]) 
298    def __init__(self, properties: List[Property]):
299        self.properties = properties
properties: List[Property]
struct_tag: str = '0x4::property_map::PropertyMap'
def to_tuple(self) -> Tuple[List[str], List[str], List[bytes]]: 
310    def to_tuple(self) -> Tuple[List[str], List[str], List[bytes]]:
311        names = []
312        types = []
313        values = []
314
315        for prop in self.properties:
316            names.append(prop.name)
317            types.append(prop.property_type)
318            values.append(prop.serialize_value())
319
320        return (names, types, values)
@staticmethod
def parse( resource: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> PropertyMap: 
322    @staticmethod
323    def parse(resource: dict[str, Any]) -> PropertyMap:
324        props = resource["inner"]["data"]
325        properties = []
326        for prop in props:
327            properties.append(
328                Property.parse(
329                    prop["key"],
330                    prop["value"]["type"],
331                    bytes.fromhex(prop["value"]["value"][2:]),
332                )
333            )
334
335        return PropertyMap(properties)
class ReadObject: 
338class ReadObject:
339    resource_map: dict[str, Any] = {
340        Collection.struct_tag: Collection,
341        Object.struct_tag: Object,
342        PropertyMap.struct_tag: PropertyMap,
343        Royalty.struct_tag: Royalty,
344        Token.struct_tag: Token,
345    }
346
347    resources: dict[Any, Any]
348
349    def __init__(self, resources: dict[Any, Any]):
350        self.resources = resources
351
352    def __str__(self) -> str:
353        response = "ReadObject"
354        for resource_obj, value in self.resources.items():
355            response += f"\n\t{resource_obj.struct_tag}: {value}"
356
357        return response
ReadObject(resources: dict[typing.Any, typing.Any]) 
349    def __init__(self, resources: dict[Any, Any]):
350        self.resources = resources
resource_map: dict[str, typing.Any] = {'0x4::collection::Collection': <class 'aptos_sdk.aptos_token_client.Collection'>, '0x1::object::ObjectCore': <class 'Object'>, '0x4::property_map::PropertyMap': <class 'PropertyMap'>, '0x4::royalty::Royalty': <class 'aptos_sdk.aptos_token_client.Royalty'>, '0x4::token::Token': <class 'aptos_sdk.aptos_token_client.Token'>}
resources: dict[typing.Any, typing.Any]
class AccountSequenceNumber: 
 42class AccountSequenceNumber:
 43    """
 44    A managed wrapper around sequence numbers that implements the trivial flow control used by the
 45    Aptos faucet:
 46    * Submit up to 100 transactions per account in parallel with a timeout of 20 seconds
 47    * If local assumes 100 are in flight, determine the actual committed state from the network
 48    * If there are less than 100 due to some being committed, adjust the window
 49    * If 100 are in flight Wait .1 seconds before re-evaluating
 50    * If ever waiting more than 30 seconds restart the sequence number to the current on-chain state
 51    Assumptions:
 52    * Accounts are expected to be managed by a single AccountSequenceNumber and not used otherwise.
 53    * They are initialized to the current on-chain state, so if there are already transactions in
 54      flight, they may take some time to reset.
 55    * Accounts are automatically initialized if not explicitly
 56
 57    Notes:
 58    * This is co-routine safe, that is many async tasks can be reading from this concurrently.
 59    * The state of an account cannot be used across multiple AccountSequenceNumber services.
 60    * The synchronize method will create a barrier that prevents additional next_sequence_number
 61      calls until it is complete.
 62    * This only manages the distribution of sequence numbers it does not help handle transaction
 63      failures.
 64    * If a transaction fails, you should call synchronize and wait for timeouts.
 65    * Mempool limits the number of transactions per account to 100, hence why we chose 100.
 66    """
 67
 68    _client: RestClient
 69    _account: AccountAddress
 70    _lock: asyncio.Lock
 71
 72    _maximum_in_flight: int = 100
 73    _maximum_wait_time: int = 30
 74    _sleep_time: float = 0.01
 75
 76    _last_committed_number: int = 0
 77    _current_number: int = 0
 78    _initialized = False
 79
 80    def __init__(
 81        self,
 82        client: RestClient,
 83        account: AccountAddress,
 84        config: AccountSequenceNumberConfig = AccountSequenceNumberConfig(),
 85    ):
 86        self._client = client
 87        self._account = account
 88        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
 89
 90        self._maximum_in_flight = config.maximum_in_flight
 91        self._maximum_wait_time = config.maximum_wait_time
 92        self._sleep_time = config.sleep_time
 93
 94    async def next_sequence_number(self, block: bool = True) -> Optional[int]:
 95        """
 96        Returns the next sequence number available on this account. This leverages a lock to
 97        guarantee first-in, first-out ordering of requests.
 98        """
 99        async with self._lock:
100            if not self._initialized:
101                await self._initialize()
102            # If there are more than self._maximum_in_flight in flight, wait for a slot.
103            # Or at least check to see if there is a slot and exit if in non-blocking mode.
104            if (
105                self._current_number - self._last_uncommitted_number
106                >= self._maximum_in_flight
107            ):
108                await self._update()
109                if (
110                    self._current_number - self._last_uncommitted_number
111                    >= self._maximum_in_flight
112                ):
113                    if not block:
114                        return None
115                    await self._resync(
116                        lambda acn: acn._current_number - acn._last_uncommitted_number
117                        >= acn._maximum_in_flight
118                    )
119
120            next_number = self._current_number
121            self._current_number += 1
122        return next_number
123
124    async def _initialize(self):
125        """Optional initializer. called by next_sequence_number if not called prior."""
126        self._initialized = True
127        self._current_number = await self._current_sequence_number()
128        self._last_uncommitted_number = self._current_number
129
130    async def synchronize(self):
131        """
132        Poll the network until all submitted transactions have either been committed or until
133        the maximum wait time has elapsed. This will prevent any calls to next_sequence_number
134        until this called has returned.
135        """
136        async with self._lock:
137            await self._update()
138            await self._resync(
139                lambda acn: acn._last_uncommitted_number != acn._current_number
140            )
141
142    async def _resync(self, check: Callable[[AccountSequenceNumber], bool]):
143        """Forces a resync with the upstream, this should be called within the lock"""
144        start_time = await self._client.current_timestamp()
145        failed = False
146        while check(self):
147            ledger_time = await self._client.current_timestamp()
148            if ledger_time - start_time > self._maximum_wait_time:
149                logging.warn(
150                    f"Waited over {self._maximum_wait_time} seconds for a transaction to commit, resyncing {self._account}"
151                )
152                failed = True
153                break
154            else:
155                await asyncio.sleep(self._sleep_time)
156                await self._update()
157        if not failed:
158            return
159        for seq_num in range(self._last_uncommitted_number + 1, self._current_number):
160            while True:
161                try:
162                    result = (
163                        await self._client.account_transaction_sequence_number_status(
164                            self._account, seq_num
165                        )
166                    )
167                    if result:
168                        break
169                except ApiError as error:
170                    if error.status_code == 404:
171                        break
172                    raise
173        await self._initialize()
174
175    async def _update(self):
176        self._last_uncommitted_number = await self._current_sequence_number()
177        return self._last_uncommitted_number
178
179    async def _current_sequence_number(self) -> int:
180        return await self._client.account_sequence_number(self._account)

A managed wrapper around sequence numbers that implements the trivial flow control used by the Aptos faucet:

  • Submit up to 100 transactions per account in parallel with a timeout of 20 seconds
  • If local assumes 100 are in flight, determine the actual committed state from the network
  • If there are less than 100 due to some being committed, adjust the window
  • If 100 are in flight Wait .1 seconds before re-evaluating
  • If ever waiting more than 30 seconds restart the sequence number to the current on-chain state Assumptions:
  • Accounts are expected to be managed by a single AccountSequenceNumber and not used otherwise.
  • They are initialized to the current on-chain state, so if there are already transactions in flight, they may take some time to reset.
  • Accounts are automatically initialized if not explicitly

Notes:

  • This is co-routine safe, that is many async tasks can be reading from this concurrently.
  • The state of an account cannot be used across multiple AccountSequenceNumber services.
  • The synchronize method will create a barrier that prevents additional next_sequence_number calls until it is complete.
  • This only manages the distribution of sequence numbers it does not help handle transaction failures.
  • If a transaction fails, you should call synchronize and wait for timeouts.
  • Mempool limits the number of transactions per account to 100, hence why we chose 100.
AccountSequenceNumber( client: RestClient, account: AccountAddress, config: aptos_sdk.account_sequence_number.AccountSequenceNumberConfig = AccountSequenceNumberConfig(maximum_in_flight=100, maximum_wait_time=30, sleep_time=0.01)) 
80    def __init__(
81        self,
82        client: RestClient,
83        account: AccountAddress,
84        config: AccountSequenceNumberConfig = AccountSequenceNumberConfig(),
85    ):
86        self._client = client
87        self._account = account
88        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
89
90        self._maximum_in_flight = config.maximum_in_flight
91        self._maximum_wait_time = config.maximum_wait_time
92        self._sleep_time = config.sleep_time
async def next_sequence_number(self, block: bool = True) -> Optional[int]: 
 94    async def next_sequence_number(self, block: bool = True) -> Optional[int]:
 95        """
 96        Returns the next sequence number available on this account. This leverages a lock to
 97        guarantee first-in, first-out ordering of requests.
 98        """
 99        async with self._lock:
100            if not self._initialized:
101                await self._initialize()
102            # If there are more than self._maximum_in_flight in flight, wait for a slot.
103            # Or at least check to see if there is a slot and exit if in non-blocking mode.
104            if (
105                self._current_number - self._last_uncommitted_number
106                >= self._maximum_in_flight
107            ):
108                await self._update()
109                if (
110                    self._current_number - self._last_uncommitted_number
111                    >= self._maximum_in_flight
112                ):
113                    if not block:
114                        return None
115                    await self._resync(
116                        lambda acn: acn._current_number - acn._last_uncommitted_number
117                        >= acn._maximum_in_flight
118                    )
119
120            next_number = self._current_number
121            self._current_number += 1
122        return next_number

Returns the next sequence number available on this account. This leverages a lock to guarantee first-in, first-out ordering of requests.

async def synchronize(self): 
130    async def synchronize(self):
131        """
132        Poll the network until all submitted transactions have either been committed or until
133        the maximum wait time has elapsed. This will prevent any calls to next_sequence_number
134        until this called has returned.
135        """
136        async with self._lock:
137            await self._update()
138            await self._resync(
139                lambda acn: acn._last_uncommitted_number != acn._current_number
140            )

Poll the network until all submitted transactions have either been committed or until the maximum wait time has elapsed. This will prevent any calls to next_sequence_number until this called has returned.

class TransactionWorker: 
 17class TransactionWorker:
 18    """
 19    The TransactionWorker provides a simple framework for receiving payloads to be processed. It
 20    acquires new sequence numbers and calls into the callback to produce a signed transaction, and
 21    then submits the transaction. In another task, it waits for resolution of the submission
 22    process or get pre-execution validation error.
 23
 24    Note: This is not a particularly robust solution, as it lacks any framework to handle failed
 25    transactions with functionality like retries or checking whether the framework is online.
 26    This is the responsibility of a higher-level framework.
 27    """
 28
 29    _account: Account
 30    _account_sequence_number: AccountSequenceNumber
 31    _rest_client: RestClient
 32    _transaction_generator: typing.Callable[
 33        [Account, int], typing.Awaitable[SignedTransaction]
 34    ]
 35    _started: bool
 36    _stopped: bool
 37    _outstanding_transactions: asyncio.Queue
 38    _outstanding_transactions_task: typing.Optional[asyncio.Task]
 39    _processed_transactions: asyncio.Queue
 40    _process_transactions_task: typing.Optional[asyncio.Task]
 41
 42    def __init__(
 43        self,
 44        account: Account,
 45        rest_client: RestClient,
 46        transaction_generator: typing.Callable[
 47            [Account, int], typing.Awaitable[SignedTransaction]
 48        ],
 49    ):
 50        self._account = account
 51        self._account_sequence_number = AccountSequenceNumber(
 52            rest_client, account.address()
 53        )
 54        self._account_sequence_number._maximum_wait_time = (
 55            rest_client.client_config.transaction_wait_in_seconds
 56        )
 57        self._rest_client = rest_client
 58        self._transaction_generator = transaction_generator
 59
 60        self._started = False
 61        self._stopped = False
 62        self._outstanding_transactions = asyncio.Queue()
 63        self._processed_transactions = asyncio.Queue()
 64
 65    def address(self) -> AccountAddress:
 66        return self._account.address()
 67
 68    async def _submit_transactions(self):
 69        try:
 70            while True:
 71                sequence_number = (
 72                    await self._account_sequence_number.next_sequence_number()
 73                )
 74                transaction = await self._transaction_generator(
 75                    self._account, sequence_number
 76                )
 77                txn_hash_awaitable = self._rest_client.submit_bcs_transaction(
 78                    transaction
 79                )
 80                await self._outstanding_transactions.put(
 81                    (txn_hash_awaitable, sequence_number)
 82                )
 83        except asyncio.CancelledError:
 84            return
 85        except Exception as e:
 86            # This is insufficient, if we hit this we either need to bail or resolve the potential errors
 87            logging.error(e, exc_info=True)
 88
 89    async def _process_transactions(self):
 90        try:
 91            while True:
 92                # Always start waiting for one, that way we can acquire a batch in the loop below.
 93                (
 94                    txn_hash_awaitable,
 95                    sequence_number,
 96                ) = await self._outstanding_transactions.get()
 97                awaitables = [txn_hash_awaitable]
 98                sequence_numbers = [sequence_number]
 99
100                # Now acquire our batch.
101                while not self._outstanding_transactions.empty():
102                    (
103                        txn_hash_awaitable,
104                        sequence_number,
105                    ) = await self._outstanding_transactions.get()
106                    awaitables.append(txn_hash_awaitable)
107                    sequence_numbers.append(sequence_number)
108
109                outputs = await asyncio.gather(*awaitables, return_exceptions=True)
110
111                for output, sequence_number in zip(outputs, sequence_numbers):
112                    if isinstance(output, BaseException):
113                        await self._processed_transactions.put(
114                            (sequence_number, None, output)
115                        )
116                    else:
117                        await self._processed_transactions.put(
118                            (sequence_number, output, None)
119                        )
120        except asyncio.CancelledError:
121            return
122        except Exception as e:
123            # This is insufficient, if we hit this we either need to bail or resolve the potential errors
124            logging.error(e, exc_info=True)
125
126    async def next_processed_transaction(
127        self,
128    ) -> typing.Tuple[int, typing.Optional[str], typing.Optional[Exception]]:
129        return await self._processed_transactions.get()
130
131    def stop(self):
132        """Stop the tasks for managing transactions"""
133        if not self._started:
134            raise ConfigurationError("TransactionWorker.start() not yet called")
135        if self._stopped:
136            raise ConfigurationError("TransactionWorker already stopped")
137        self._stopped = True
138
139        self._submit_transactions_task.cancel()
140        self._process_transactions_task.cancel()
141
142    def start(self):
143        """Begin the tasks for managing transactions"""
144        if self._started:
145            raise ConfigurationError("TransactionWorker already started")
146        self._started = True
147
148        self._submit_transactions_task = asyncio.create_task(
149            self._submit_transactions()
150        )
151        self._process_transactions_task = asyncio.create_task(
152            self._process_transactions()
153        )

The TransactionWorker provides a simple framework for receiving payloads to be processed. It acquires new sequence numbers and calls into the callback to produce a signed transaction, and then submits the transaction. In another task, it waits for resolution of the submission process or get pre-execution validation error.

Note: This is not a particularly robust solution, as it lacks any framework to handle failed transactions with functionality like retries or checking whether the framework is online. This is the responsibility of a higher-level framework.

TransactionWorker( account: Account, rest_client: RestClient, transaction_generator: Callable[[Account, int], Awaitable[SignedTransaction]]) 
42    def __init__(
43        self,
44        account: Account,
45        rest_client: RestClient,
46        transaction_generator: typing.Callable[
47            [Account, int], typing.Awaitable[SignedTransaction]
48        ],
49    ):
50        self._account = account
51        self._account_sequence_number = AccountSequenceNumber(
52            rest_client, account.address()
53        )
54        self._account_sequence_number._maximum_wait_time = (
55            rest_client.client_config.transaction_wait_in_seconds
56        )
57        self._rest_client = rest_client
58        self._transaction_generator = transaction_generator
59
60        self._started = False
61        self._stopped = False
62        self._outstanding_transactions = asyncio.Queue()
63        self._processed_transactions = asyncio.Queue()
def address(self) -> AccountAddress: 
65    def address(self) -> AccountAddress:
66        return self._account.address()
async def next_processed_transaction(self) -> Tuple[int, Optional[str], Optional[Exception]]: 
126    async def next_processed_transaction(
127        self,
128    ) -> typing.Tuple[int, typing.Optional[str], typing.Optional[Exception]]:
129        return await self._processed_transactions.get()
def stop(self): 
131    def stop(self):
132        """Stop the tasks for managing transactions"""
133        if not self._started:
134            raise ConfigurationError("TransactionWorker.start() not yet called")
135        if self._stopped:
136            raise ConfigurationError("TransactionWorker already stopped")
137        self._stopped = True
138
139        self._submit_transactions_task.cancel()
140        self._process_transactions_task.cancel()

Stop the tasks for managing transactions

def start(self): 
142    def start(self):
143        """Begin the tasks for managing transactions"""
144        if self._started:
145            raise ConfigurationError("TransactionWorker already started")
146        self._started = True
147
148        self._submit_transactions_task = asyncio.create_task(
149            self._submit_transactions()
150        )
151        self._process_transactions_task = asyncio.create_task(
152            self._process_transactions()
153        )

Begin the tasks for managing transactions

class PackagePublisher: 
 35class PackagePublisher:
 36    """A wrapper around publishing packages."""
 37
 38    client: RestClient
 39
 40    def __init__(self, client: RestClient):
 41        self.client = client
 42
 43    async def publish_package(
 44        self, sender: Account, package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes]
 45    ) -> str:
 46        transaction_arguments = [
 47            TransactionArgument(package_metadata, Serializer.to_bytes),
 48            TransactionArgument(
 49                modules, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
 50            ),
 51        ]
 52
 53        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
 54            "0x1::code",
 55            "publish_package_txn",
 56            [],
 57            transaction_arguments,
 58        )
 59
 60        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
 61            sender, TransactionPayload(payload)
 62        )
 63        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
 64
 65    async def publish_package_to_object(
 66        self, sender: Account, package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes]
 67    ) -> str:
 68        transaction_arguments = [
 69            TransactionArgument(package_metadata, Serializer.to_bytes),
 70            TransactionArgument(
 71                modules, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
 72            ),
 73        ]
 74
 75        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
 76            "0x1::object_code_deployment",
 77            "publish",
 78            [],
 79            transaction_arguments,
 80        )
 81
 82        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
 83            sender, TransactionPayload(payload)
 84        )
 85        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
 86
 87    async def upgrade_package_object(
 88        self,
 89        sender: Account,
 90        package_metadata: bytes,
 91        modules: List[bytes],
 92        object_address: AccountAddress,
 93    ) -> str:
 94        transaction_arguments = [
 95            TransactionArgument(package_metadata, Serializer.to_bytes),
 96            TransactionArgument(
 97                modules, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
 98            ),
 99            TransactionArgument(object_address, Serializer.struct),
100        ]
101
102        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
103            "0x1::object_code_deployment",
104            "upgrade",
105            [],
106            transaction_arguments,
107        )
108
109        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
110            sender, TransactionPayload(payload)
111        )
112        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
113
114    async def publish_package_in_path(
115        self,
116        sender: Account,
117        package_dir: str,
118        large_package_address: AccountAddress = MODULE_ADDRESS,
119        publish_mode: PublishMode = PublishMode.ACCOUNT_DEPLOY,
120        code_object: Optional[AccountAddress] = None,
121    ) -> List[str]:
122        with open(os.path.join(package_dir, "Move.toml"), "rb") as f:
123            data = tomli.load(f)
124        package = data["package"]["name"]
125
126        package_build_dir = os.path.join(package_dir, "build", package)
127        module_directory = os.path.join(package_build_dir, "bytecode_modules")
128        module_paths = os.listdir(module_directory)
129        modules = []
130        for module_path in module_paths:
131            module_path = os.path.join(module_directory, module_path)
132            if not os.path.isfile(module_path) and not module_path.endswith(".mv"):
133                continue
134            with open(module_path, "rb") as f:
135                module = f.read()
136                modules.append(module)
137
138        metadata_path = os.path.join(package_build_dir, "package-metadata.bcs")
139        with open(metadata_path, "rb") as f:
140            metadata = f.read()
141
142        # If the package size is larger than a single transaction limit, use chunked publish.
143        if self.is_large_package(metadata, modules):
144            return await self.chunked_package_publish(
145                sender, metadata, modules, large_package_address, publish_mode
146            )
147
148        # If the deployment can fit into a single transaction, use the normal package publisher
149        if publish_mode == PublishMode.ACCOUNT_DEPLOY:
150            txn_hash = await self.publish_package(sender, metadata, modules)
151        elif publish_mode == PublishMode.OBJECT_DEPLOY:
152            txn_hash = await self.publish_package_to_object(sender, metadata, modules)
153        elif publish_mode == PublishMode.OBJECT_UPGRADE:
154            if code_object is None:
155                raise ValueError("code_object must be provided for OBJECT_UPGRADE mode")
156            txn_hash = await self.upgrade_package_object(
157                sender, metadata, modules, code_object
158            )
159        else:
160            raise ValueError(f"Unexpected publish mode: {publish_mode}")
161
162        return [txn_hash]
163
164    async def derive_object_address(
165        self, publisher_address: AccountAddress
166    ) -> AccountAddress:
167        sequence_number = await self.client.account_sequence_number(publisher_address)
168        return self.create_object_deployment_address(
169            publisher_address, sequence_number + 1
170        )
171
172    @staticmethod
173    def create_object_deployment_address(
174        creator_address: AccountAddress, creator_sequence_number: int
175    ) -> AccountAddress:
176        ser = Serializer()
177        ser.to_bytes(OBJECT_CODE_DEPLOYMENT_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR)
178        ser.u64(creator_sequence_number)
179        seed = ser.output()
180
181        return AccountAddress.for_named_object(creator_address, seed)
182
183    async def chunked_package_publish(
184        self,
185        sender: Account,
186        package_metadata: bytes,
187        modules: List[bytes],
188        large_package_address: AccountAddress = MODULE_ADDRESS,
189        publish_mode: PublishMode = PublishMode.ACCOUNT_DEPLOY,
190    ) -> List[str]:
191        """
192        Chunks the package_metadata and modules across as many transactions as necessary.
193        Each transaction has a base cost and the maximum size is currently 64K, so this chunks
194        them into 62K + the base transaction size. This should be sufficient for reasonably
195        optimistic transaction batching. The batching tries to place as much data in a transaction
196        before moving to the chunk to the next transaction.
197        """
198
199        # Chunk the metadata and insert it into payloads. The last chunk may be small enough
200        # to be placed with other data. This may also be the only chunk.
201        payloads = []
202        metadata_chunks = PackagePublisher.create_chunks(package_metadata)
203        for metadata_chunk in metadata_chunks[:-1]:
204            payloads.append(
205                PackagePublisher.create_large_package_publishing_payload(
206                    large_package_address, metadata_chunk, [], [], False
207                )
208            )
209
210        metadata_chunk = metadata_chunks[-1]
211        taken_size = len(metadata_chunk)
212        modules_indices: List[int] = []
213        data_chunks: List[bytes] = []
214
215        # Chunk each module and place them into a payload when adding more would exceed the
216        # maximum transaction size.
217        for idx, module in enumerate(modules):
218            chunked_module = PackagePublisher.create_chunks(module)
219            for chunk in chunked_module:
220                if taken_size + len(chunk) > MAX_TRANSACTION_SIZE:
221                    payloads.append(
222                        PackagePublisher.create_large_package_publishing_payload(
223                            large_package_address,
224                            metadata_chunk,
225                            modules_indices,
226                            data_chunks,
227                            False,
228                        )
229                    )
230                    metadata_chunk = b""
231                    modules_indices = []
232                    data_chunks = []
233                    taken_size = 0
234                if idx not in modules_indices:
235                    modules_indices.append(idx)
236                data_chunks.append(chunk)
237                taken_size += len(chunk)
238
239        # There will almost certainly be left over data from the chunking, so pass the last
240        # chunk for the sake of publishing.
241        payloads.append(
242            PackagePublisher.create_large_package_publishing_payload(
243                large_package_address,
244                metadata_chunk,
245                modules_indices,
246                data_chunks,
247                True,
248            )
249        )
250
251        # Submit and wait for each transaction, including publishing.
252        txn_hashes = []
253        for payload in payloads:
254            print("Submitting transaction...")
255            signed_txn = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
256                sender, payload
257            )
258            txn_hash = await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_txn)
259            await self.client.wait_for_transaction(txn_hash)
260            txn_hashes.append(txn_hash)
261        return txn_hashes
262
263    @staticmethod
264    def create_large_package_publishing_payload(
265        module_address: AccountAddress,
266        chunked_package_metadata: bytes,
267        modules_indices: List[int],
268        chunked_modules: List[bytes],
269        publish: bool,
270    ) -> TransactionPayload:
271        transaction_arguments = [
272            TransactionArgument(chunked_package_metadata, Serializer.to_bytes),
273            TransactionArgument(
274                modules_indices, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.u16)
275            ),
276            TransactionArgument(
277                chunked_modules, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
278            ),
279            TransactionArgument(publish, Serializer.bool),
280        ]
281
282        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
283            f"{module_address}::large_packages",
284            "stage_code",
285            [],
286            transaction_arguments,
287        )
288
289        return TransactionPayload(payload)
290
291    @staticmethod
292    def is_large_package(
293        package_metadata: bytes,
294        modules: List[bytes],
295    ) -> bool:
296        total_size = len(package_metadata)
297        for module in modules:
298            total_size += len(module)
299
300        return total_size >= MAX_TRANSACTION_SIZE
301
302    @staticmethod
303    def create_chunks(data: bytes) -> List[bytes]:
304        chunks: List[bytes] = []
305        read_data = 0
306        while read_data < len(data):
307            start_read_data = read_data
308            read_data = min(read_data + MAX_TRANSACTION_SIZE, len(data))
309            taken_data = data[start_read_data:read_data]
310            chunks.append(taken_data)
311        return chunks

A wrapper around publishing packages.

PackagePublisher(client: RestClient) 
40    def __init__(self, client: RestClient):
41        self.client = client
client: RestClient
async def publish_package( self, sender: Account, package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes]) -> str: 
43    async def publish_package(
44        self, sender: Account, package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes]
45    ) -> str:
46        transaction_arguments = [
47            TransactionArgument(package_metadata, Serializer.to_bytes),
48            TransactionArgument(
49                modules, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
50            ),
51        ]
52
53        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
54            "0x1::code",
55            "publish_package_txn",
56            [],
57            transaction_arguments,
58        )
59
60        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
61            sender, TransactionPayload(payload)
62        )
63        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def publish_package_to_object( self, sender: Account, package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes]) -> str: 
65    async def publish_package_to_object(
66        self, sender: Account, package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes]
67    ) -> str:
68        transaction_arguments = [
69            TransactionArgument(package_metadata, Serializer.to_bytes),
70            TransactionArgument(
71                modules, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
72            ),
73        ]
74
75        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
76            "0x1::object_code_deployment",
77            "publish",
78            [],
79            transaction_arguments,
80        )
81
82        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
83            sender, TransactionPayload(payload)
84        )
85        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def upgrade_package_object( self, sender: Account, package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes], object_address: AccountAddress) -> str: 
 87    async def upgrade_package_object(
 88        self,
 89        sender: Account,
 90        package_metadata: bytes,
 91        modules: List[bytes],
 92        object_address: AccountAddress,
 93    ) -> str:
 94        transaction_arguments = [
 95            TransactionArgument(package_metadata, Serializer.to_bytes),
 96            TransactionArgument(
 97                modules, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
 98            ),
 99            TransactionArgument(object_address, Serializer.struct),
100        ]
101
102        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
103            "0x1::object_code_deployment",
104            "upgrade",
105            [],
106            transaction_arguments,
107        )
108
109        signed_transaction = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
110            sender, TransactionPayload(payload)
111        )
112        return await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_transaction)
async def publish_package_in_path( self, sender: Account, package_dir: str, large_package_address: AccountAddress = 0xfa3911d7715238b2e3bd5b26b6a35e11ffa16cff318bc11471e84eccee8bd291, publish_mode: aptos_sdk.package_publisher.PublishMode = <PublishMode.ACCOUNT_DEPLOY: 'ACCOUNT_DEPLOY'>, code_object: Optional[AccountAddress] = None) -> List[str]: 
114    async def publish_package_in_path(
115        self,
116        sender: Account,
117        package_dir: str,
118        large_package_address: AccountAddress = MODULE_ADDRESS,
119        publish_mode: PublishMode = PublishMode.ACCOUNT_DEPLOY,
120        code_object: Optional[AccountAddress] = None,
121    ) -> List[str]:
122        with open(os.path.join(package_dir, "Move.toml"), "rb") as f:
123            data = tomli.load(f)
124        package = data["package"]["name"]
125
126        package_build_dir = os.path.join(package_dir, "build", package)
127        module_directory = os.path.join(package_build_dir, "bytecode_modules")
128        module_paths = os.listdir(module_directory)
129        modules = []
130        for module_path in module_paths:
131            module_path = os.path.join(module_directory, module_path)
132            if not os.path.isfile(module_path) and not module_path.endswith(".mv"):
133                continue
134            with open(module_path, "rb") as f:
135                module = f.read()
136                modules.append(module)
137
138        metadata_path = os.path.join(package_build_dir, "package-metadata.bcs")
139        with open(metadata_path, "rb") as f:
140            metadata = f.read()
141
142        # If the package size is larger than a single transaction limit, use chunked publish.
143        if self.is_large_package(metadata, modules):
144            return await self.chunked_package_publish(
145                sender, metadata, modules, large_package_address, publish_mode
146            )
147
