148        # If the deployment can fit into a single transaction, use the normal package publisher
149        if publish_mode == PublishMode.ACCOUNT_DEPLOY:
150            txn_hash = await self.publish_package(sender, metadata, modules)
151        elif publish_mode == PublishMode.OBJECT_DEPLOY:
152            txn_hash = await self.publish_package_to_object(sender, metadata, modules)
153        elif publish_mode == PublishMode.OBJECT_UPGRADE:
154            if code_object is None:
155                raise ValueError("code_object must be provided for OBJECT_UPGRADE mode")
156            txn_hash = await self.upgrade_package_object(
157                sender, metadata, modules, code_object
158            )
159        else:
160            raise ValueError(f"Unexpected publish mode: {publish_mode}")
161
162        return [txn_hash]
async def derive_object_address( self, publisher_address: AccountAddress) -> AccountAddress: 
164    async def derive_object_address(
165        self, publisher_address: AccountAddress
166    ) -> AccountAddress:
167        sequence_number = await self.client.account_sequence_number(publisher_address)
168        return self.create_object_deployment_address(
169            publisher_address, sequence_number + 1
170        )
@staticmethod
def create_object_deployment_address( creator_address: AccountAddress, creator_sequence_number: int) -> AccountAddress: 
172    @staticmethod
173    def create_object_deployment_address(
174        creator_address: AccountAddress, creator_sequence_number: int
175    ) -> AccountAddress:
176        ser = Serializer()
177        ser.to_bytes(OBJECT_CODE_DEPLOYMENT_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR)
178        ser.u64(creator_sequence_number)
179        seed = ser.output()
180
181        return AccountAddress.for_named_object(creator_address, seed)
async def chunked_package_publish( self, sender: Account, package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes], large_package_address: AccountAddress = 0xfa3911d7715238b2e3bd5b26b6a35e11ffa16cff318bc11471e84eccee8bd291, publish_mode: aptos_sdk.package_publisher.PublishMode = <PublishMode.ACCOUNT_DEPLOY: 'ACCOUNT_DEPLOY'>) -> List[str]: 
183    async def chunked_package_publish(
184        self,
185        sender: Account,
186        package_metadata: bytes,
187        modules: List[bytes],
188        large_package_address: AccountAddress = MODULE_ADDRESS,
189        publish_mode: PublishMode = PublishMode.ACCOUNT_DEPLOY,
190    ) -> List[str]:
191        """
192        Chunks the package_metadata and modules across as many transactions as necessary.
193        Each transaction has a base cost and the maximum size is currently 64K, so this chunks
194        them into 62K + the base transaction size. This should be sufficient for reasonably
195        optimistic transaction batching. The batching tries to place as much data in a transaction
196        before moving to the chunk to the next transaction.
197        """
198
199        # Chunk the metadata and insert it into payloads. The last chunk may be small enough
200        # to be placed with other data. This may also be the only chunk.
201        payloads = []
202        metadata_chunks = PackagePublisher.create_chunks(package_metadata)
203        for metadata_chunk in metadata_chunks[:-1]:
204            payloads.append(
205                PackagePublisher.create_large_package_publishing_payload(
206                    large_package_address, metadata_chunk, [], [], False
207                )
208            )
209
210        metadata_chunk = metadata_chunks[-1]
211        taken_size = len(metadata_chunk)
212        modules_indices: List[int] = []
213        data_chunks: List[bytes] = []
214
215        # Chunk each module and place them into a payload when adding more would exceed the
216        # maximum transaction size.
217        for idx, module in enumerate(modules):
218            chunked_module = PackagePublisher.create_chunks(module)
219            for chunk in chunked_module:
220                if taken_size + len(chunk) > MAX_TRANSACTION_SIZE:
221                    payloads.append(
222                        PackagePublisher.create_large_package_publishing_payload(
223                            large_package_address,
224                            metadata_chunk,
225                            modules_indices,
226                            data_chunks,
227                            False,
228                        )
229                    )
230                    metadata_chunk = b""
231                    modules_indices = []
232                    data_chunks = []
233                    taken_size = 0
234                if idx not in modules_indices:
235                    modules_indices.append(idx)
236                data_chunks.append(chunk)
237                taken_size += len(chunk)
238
239        # There will almost certainly be left over data from the chunking, so pass the last
240        # chunk for the sake of publishing.
241        payloads.append(
242            PackagePublisher.create_large_package_publishing_payload(
243                large_package_address,
244                metadata_chunk,
245                modules_indices,
246                data_chunks,
247                True,
248            )
249        )
250
251        # Submit and wait for each transaction, including publishing.
252        txn_hashes = []
253        for payload in payloads:
254            print("Submitting transaction...")
255            signed_txn = await self.client.create_bcs_signed_transaction(
256                sender, payload
257            )
258            txn_hash = await self.client.submit_bcs_transaction(signed_txn)
259            await self.client.wait_for_transaction(txn_hash)
260            txn_hashes.append(txn_hash)
261        return txn_hashes

Chunks the package_metadata and modules across as many transactions as necessary. Each transaction has a base cost and the maximum size is currently 64K, so this chunks them into 62K + the base transaction size. This should be sufficient for reasonably optimistic transaction batching. The batching tries to place as much data in a transaction before moving to the chunk to the next transaction.

@staticmethod
def create_large_package_publishing_payload( module_address: AccountAddress, chunked_package_metadata: bytes, modules_indices: List[int], chunked_modules: List[bytes], publish: bool) -> TransactionPayload: 
263    @staticmethod
264    def create_large_package_publishing_payload(
265        module_address: AccountAddress,
266        chunked_package_metadata: bytes,
267        modules_indices: List[int],
268        chunked_modules: List[bytes],
269        publish: bool,
270    ) -> TransactionPayload:
271        transaction_arguments = [
272            TransactionArgument(chunked_package_metadata, Serializer.to_bytes),
273            TransactionArgument(
274                modules_indices, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.u16)
275            ),
276            TransactionArgument(
277                chunked_modules, Serializer.sequence_serializer(Serializer.to_bytes)
278            ),
279            TransactionArgument(publish, Serializer.bool),
280        ]
281
282        payload = EntryFunction.natural(
283            f"{module_address}::large_packages",
284            "stage_code",
285            [],
286            transaction_arguments,
287        )
288
289        return TransactionPayload(payload)
@staticmethod
def is_large_package(package_metadata: bytes, modules: List[bytes]) -> bool: 
291    @staticmethod
292    def is_large_package(
293        package_metadata: bytes,
294        modules: List[bytes],
295    ) -> bool:
296        total_size = len(package_metadata)
297        for module in modules:
298            total_size += len(module)
299
300        return total_size >= MAX_TRANSACTION_SIZE
@staticmethod
def create_chunks(data: bytes) -> List[bytes]: 
302    @staticmethod
303    def create_chunks(data: bytes) -> List[bytes]:
304        chunks: List[bytes] = []
305        read_data = 0
306        while read_data < len(data):
307            start_read_data = read_data
308            read_data = min(read_data + MAX_TRANSACTION_SIZE, len(data))
309            taken_data = data[start_read_data:read_data]
310            chunks.append(taken_data)
311        return chunks
class Network(enum.Enum): 
 46class Network(Enum):
 47    """
 48    Well-known Aptos networks with pre-configured endpoints.
 49
 50    Example:
 51        >>> from aptos_sdk import Network, RestClient
 52        >>>
 53        >>> # Use testnet
 54        >>> async with RestClient(Network.TESTNET.fullnode_url) as client:
 55        ...     info = await client.info()
 56        ...     print(f"Chain ID: {info['chain_id']}")
 57        >>>
 58        >>> # Get faucet URL
 59        >>> print(Network.DEVNET.faucet_url)
 60        'https://faucet.devnet.aptoslabs.com'
 61    """
 62
 63    MAINNET = NetworkConfig(
 64        name="mainnet",
 65        chain_id=1,
 66        fullnode_url="https://fullnode.mainnet.aptoslabs.com/v1",
 67        indexer_url="https://indexer.mainnet.aptoslabs.com/v1/graphql",
 68        faucet_url=None,  # No faucet on mainnet
 69    )
 70
 71    TESTNET = NetworkConfig(
 72        name="testnet",
 73        chain_id=2,
 74        fullnode_url="https://fullnode.testnet.aptoslabs.com/v1",
 75        indexer_url="https://indexer.testnet.aptoslabs.com/v1/graphql",
 76        faucet_url="https://faucet.testnet.aptoslabs.com",
 77    )
 78
 79    DEVNET = NetworkConfig(
 80        name="devnet",
 81        chain_id=4,
 82        fullnode_url="https://fullnode.devnet.aptoslabs.com/v1",
 83        indexer_url="https://indexer.devnet.aptoslabs.com/v1/graphql",
 84        faucet_url="https://faucet.devnet.aptoslabs.com",
 85    )
 86
 87    LOCAL = NetworkConfig(
 88        name="local",
 89        chain_id=4,  # Local networks typically use chain ID 4
 90        fullnode_url="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1",
 91        indexer_url=None,
 92        faucet_url="http://127.0.0.1:8081",
 93    )
 94
 95    @property
 96    def name(self) -> str:
 97        """Get the network name."""
 98        return self.value.name
 99
100    @property
101    def chain_id(self) -> int:
102        """Get the chain ID."""
103        return self.value.chain_id
104
105    @property
106    def fullnode_url(self) -> str:
107        """Get the fullnode REST API URL."""
108        return self.value.fullnode_url
109
110    @property
111    def indexer_url(self) -> Optional[str]:
112        """Get the indexer GraphQL URL, if available."""
113        return self.value.indexer_url
114
115    @property
116    def faucet_url(self) -> Optional[str]:
117        """Get the faucet URL, if available."""
118        return self.value.faucet_url
119
120    @classmethod
121    def from_string(cls, network: str) -> "Network":
122        """
123        Get a Network enum from a string name.
124
125        Args:
126            network: Network name (case-insensitive): "mainnet", "testnet", "devnet", "local"
127
128        Returns:
129            The corresponding Network enum value.
130
131        Raises:
132            ValueError: If the network name is not recognized.
133
134        Example:
135            >>> Network.from_string("testnet")
136            <Network.TESTNET: NetworkConfig(...)>
137            >>> Network.from_string("MAINNET")
138            <Network.MAINNET: NetworkConfig(...)>
139        """
140        name_upper = network.upper()
141        try:
142            return cls[name_upper]
143        except KeyError:
144            valid = ", ".join(n.name.lower() for n in cls)
145            raise ValueError(
146                f"Unknown network '{network}'. Valid networks: {valid}"
147            ) from None
148
149    def __str__(self) -> str:
150        """Return the lowercase network name."""
151        return self.value.name

Well-known Aptos networks with pre-configured endpoints.

Example:

from aptos_sdk import Network, RestClient

Use testnet

async with RestClient(Network.TESTNET.fullnode_url) as client: ... info = await client.info() ... print(f"Chain ID: {info['chain_id']}")

Get faucet URL

print(Network.DEVNET.faucet_url) 'https://faucet.devnet.aptoslabs.com'

MAINNET = <Network.MAINNET: NetworkConfig(name='mainnet', chain_id=1, fullnode_url='https://fullnode.mainnet.aptoslabs.com/v1', indexer_url='https://indexer.mainnet.aptoslabs.com/v1/graphql', faucet_url=None)>
TESTNET = <Network.TESTNET: NetworkConfig(name='testnet', chain_id=2, fullnode_url='https://fullnode.testnet.aptoslabs.com/v1', indexer_url='https://indexer.testnet.aptoslabs.com/v1/graphql', faucet_url='https://faucet.testnet.aptoslabs.com')>
DEVNET = <Network.DEVNET: NetworkConfig(name='devnet', chain_id=4, fullnode_url='https://fullnode.devnet.aptoslabs.com/v1', indexer_url='https://indexer.devnet.aptoslabs.com/v1/graphql', faucet_url='https://faucet.devnet.aptoslabs.com')>
LOCAL = <Network.LOCAL: NetworkConfig(name='local', chain_id=4, fullnode_url='http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1', indexer_url=None, faucet_url='http://127.0.0.1:8081')>
name: str 
95    @property
96    def name(self) -> str:
97        """Get the network name."""
98        return self.value.name

Get the network name.

chain_id: int 
100    @property
101    def chain_id(self) -> int:
102        """Get the chain ID."""
103        return self.value.chain_id

Get the chain ID.

fullnode_url: str 
105    @property
106    def fullnode_url(self) -> str:
107        """Get the fullnode REST API URL."""
108        return self.value.fullnode_url

Get the fullnode REST API URL.

indexer_url: Optional[str] 
110    @property
111    def indexer_url(self) -> Optional[str]:
112        """Get the indexer GraphQL URL, if available."""
113        return self.value.indexer_url

Get the indexer GraphQL URL, if available.

faucet_url: Optional[str] 
115    @property
116    def faucet_url(self) -> Optional[str]:
117        """Get the faucet URL, if available."""
118        return self.value.faucet_url

Get the faucet URL, if available.

@classmethod
def from_string(cls, network: str) -> Network: 
120    @classmethod
121    def from_string(cls, network: str) -> "Network":
122        """
123        Get a Network enum from a string name.
124
125        Args:
126            network: Network name (case-insensitive): "mainnet", "testnet", "devnet", "local"
127
128        Returns:
129            The corresponding Network enum value.
130
131        Raises:
132            ValueError: If the network name is not recognized.
133
134        Example:
135            >>> Network.from_string("testnet")
136            <Network.TESTNET: NetworkConfig(...)>
137            >>> Network.from_string("MAINNET")
138            <Network.MAINNET: NetworkConfig(...)>
139        """
140        name_upper = network.upper()
141        try:
142            return cls[name_upper]
143        except KeyError:
144            valid = ", ".join(n.name.lower() for n in cls)
145            raise ValueError(
146                f"Unknown network '{network}'. Valid networks: {valid}"
147            ) from None

Get a Network enum from a string name.

Args: network: Network name (case-insensitive): "mainnet", "testnet", "devnet", "local"

Returns: The corresponding Network enum value.

Raises: ValueError: If the network name is not recognized.

Example:

Network.from_string("testnet") <Network.TESTNET: NetworkConfig(...)> Network.from_string("MAINNET") <Network.MAINNET: NetworkConfig(...)>

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class NetworkConfig: 
26@dataclass(frozen=True)
27class NetworkConfig:
28    """
29    Configuration for an Aptos network.
30
31    Attributes:
32        name: Human-readable network name.
33        chain_id: The chain ID for this network.
34        fullnode_url: URL of the fullnode REST API.
35        indexer_url: URL of the indexer GraphQL API (if available).
36        faucet_url: URL of the faucet service (if available).
37    """
38
39    name: str
40    chain_id: int
41    fullnode_url: str
42    indexer_url: Optional[str] = None
43    faucet_url: Optional[str] = None

Configuration for an Aptos network.

Attributes: name: Human-readable network name. chain_id: The chain ID for this network. fullnode_url: URL of the fullnode REST API. indexer_url: URL of the indexer GraphQL API (if available). faucet_url: URL of the faucet service (if available).

NetworkConfig( name: str, chain_id: int, fullnode_url: str, indexer_url: Optional[str] = None, faucet_url: Optional[str] = None)
name: str
chain_id: int
fullnode_url: str
indexer_url: Optional[str] = None
faucet_url: Optional[str] = None
MAINNET_URL = 'https://fullnode.mainnet.aptoslabs.com/v1'
TESTNET_URL = 'https://fullnode.testnet.aptoslabs.com/v1'
DEVNET_URL = 'https://fullnode.devnet.aptoslabs.com/v1'
@dataclass
class FungibleAssetMetadata: 
28@dataclass
29class FungibleAssetMetadata:
30    """
31    Metadata for a Fungible Asset.
32
33    Attributes:
34        name: Human-readable name of the asset.
35        symbol: Trading symbol (e.g., "APT", "USDC").
36        decimals: Number of decimal places.
37        icon_uri: Optional URI for the asset icon.
38        project_uri: Optional URI for the project website.
39    """
40
41    name: str
42    symbol: str
43    decimals: int
44    icon_uri: Optional[str] = None
45    project_uri: Optional[str] = None

Metadata for a Fungible Asset.

Attributes: name: Human-readable name of the asset. symbol: Trading symbol (e.g., "APT", "USDC"). decimals: Number of decimal places. icon_uri: Optional URI for the asset icon. project_uri: Optional URI for the project website.

FungibleAssetMetadata( name: str, symbol: str, decimals: int, icon_uri: Optional[str] = None, project_uri: Optional[str] = None)
name: str
symbol: str
decimals: int
icon_uri: Optional[str] = None
project_uri: Optional[str] = None
class AccountNotFound(aptos_sdk.ApiError): 
55class AccountNotFound(ApiError):
56    """
57    The specified account was not found on chain.
58
59    This typically means the account has not been created yet (no transactions
60    have been sent to or from it).
61
62    Attributes:
63        address: The account address that was not found.
64    """
65
66    def __init__(self, address: "AccountAddress"):
67        self.address = address
68        super().__init__(
69            f"Account not found: {address}. The account may not exist on chain yet.",
70            404,
71        )

The specified account was not found on chain.

This typically means the account has not been created yet (no transactions have been sent to or from it).

Attributes: address: The account address that was not found.

AccountNotFound(address: AccountAddress) 
66    def __init__(self, address: "AccountAddress"):
67        self.address = address
68        super().__init__(
69            f"Account not found: {address}. The account may not exist on chain yet.",
70            404,
71        )
address
class AddressError(aptos_sdk.AptosError): 
225class AddressError(AptosError):
226    """Base class for account address errors."""

Base class for account address errors.

class ApiError(aptos_sdk.AptosError): 
37class ApiError(AptosError):
38    """
39    The API returned a non-success status code.
40
41    Attributes:
42        message: Human-readable error description.
43        status_code: HTTP status code from the API response.
44    """
45
46    def __init__(self, message: str, status_code: int):
47        self.message = message
48        self.status_code = status_code
49        super().__init__(f"API Error ({status_code}): {message}")
50
51    def __repr__(self) -> str:
52        return f"ApiError(message={self.message!r}, status_code={self.status_code})"

The API returned a non-success status code.

Attributes: message: Human-readable error description. status_code: HTTP status code from the API response.

ApiError(message: str, status_code: int) 
46    def __init__(self, message: str, status_code: int):
47        self.message = message
48        self.status_code = status_code
49        super().__init__(f"API Error ({status_code}): {message}")
message
status_code
class AptosError(builtins.Exception): 
28class AptosError(Exception):
29    """Base exception for all Aptos SDK errors."""

Base exception for all Aptos SDK errors.

class BcsError(aptos_sdk.AptosError): 
156class BcsError(AptosError):
157    """Base class for BCS serialization/deserialization errors."""

Base class for BCS serialization/deserialization errors.

class ConfigurationError(aptos_sdk.AptosError): 
243class ConfigurationError(AptosError):
244    """Invalid SDK configuration."""
245
246    def __init__(self, message: str = "Invalid configuration"):
247        super().__init__(message)

Invalid SDK configuration.

ConfigurationError(message: str = 'Invalid configuration') 
246    def __init__(self, message: str = "Invalid configuration"):
247        super().__init__(message)
class CryptoError(aptos_sdk.AptosError): 
195class CryptoError(AptosError):
196    """Base class for cryptographic operation errors."""

Base class for cryptographic operation errors.

class DeserializationError(aptos_sdk.BcsError): 
160class DeserializationError(BcsError):
161    """
162    Failed to deserialize BCS data.
163
164    Attributes:
165        message: Description of what went wrong.
166        position: Optional byte position where the error occurred.
167    """
168
169    def __init__(self, message: str, position: Optional[int] = None):
170        self.position = position
171        pos_str = f" at position {position}" if position is not None else ""
172        super().__init__(f"BCS deserialization error{pos_str}: {message}")

Failed to deserialize BCS data.

Attributes: message: Description of what went wrong. position: Optional byte position where the error occurred.

DeserializationError(message: str, position: Optional[int] = None) 
169    def __init__(self, message: str, position: Optional[int] = None):
170        self.position = position
171        pos_str = f" at position {position}" if position is not None else ""
172        super().__init__(f"BCS deserialization error{pos_str}: {message}")
position
class InvalidAddress(aptos_sdk.AddressError): 
229class InvalidAddress(AddressError):
230    """The provided address string is invalid."""
231
232    def __init__(self, address_str: str, reason: Optional[str] = None):
233        self.address_str = address_str
234        reason_str = f": {reason}" if reason else ""
235        super().__init__(f"Invalid address '{address_str}'{reason_str}")

The provided address string is invalid.

InvalidAddress(address_str: str, reason: Optional[str] = None) 
232    def __init__(self, address_str: str, reason: Optional[str] = None):
233        self.address_str = address_str
234        reason_str = f": {reason}" if reason else ""
235        super().__init__(f"Invalid address '{address_str}'{reason_str}")
address_str

210        super().__init__(message)

The provided private key is invalid or malformed.

InvalidPrivateKey(message: str = 'Invalid private key format')

182class InvalidTypeTag(BcsError):

186        self.tag_value = tag_value
187        super().__init__(f"Invalid type tag value: {tag_value}")

class ModuleNotFound(aptos_sdk.ApiError): 
90class ModuleNotFound(ApiError):
91    """The specified module was not found on an account."""
92
93    def __init__(self, module_name: str, address: Optional["AccountAddress"] = None):
94        self.module_name = module_name
95        self.address = address
96        addr_str = f" on account {address}" if address else ""
97        super().__init__(f"Module not found: {module_name}{addr_str}", 404)

The specified module was not found on an account.

ModuleNotFound( module_name: str, address: Optional[AccountAddress] = None) 
93    def __init__(self, module_name: str, address: Optional["AccountAddress"] = None):
94        self.module_name = module_name
95        self.address = address
96        addr_str = f" on account {address}" if address else ""
97        super().__init__(f"Module not found: {module_name}{addr_str}", 404)
module_name
address
class ResourceNotFound(aptos_sdk.ApiError): 
74class ResourceNotFound(ApiError):
75    """
76    The specified resource was not found on an account.
77
78    Attributes:
79        resource_type: The Move resource type that was not found.
80        address: Optional account address where the resource was expected.
81    """
82
83    def __init__(self, resource_type: str, address: Optional["AccountAddress"] = None):
84        self.resource_type = resource_type
85        self.address = address
86        addr_str = f" on account {address}" if address else ""
87        super().__init__(f"Resource not found: {resource_type}{addr_str}", 404)

The specified resource was not found on an account.

Attributes: resource_type: The Move resource type that was not found. address: Optional account address where the resource was expected.

ResourceNotFound( resource_type: str, address: Optional[AccountAddress] = None) 
83    def __init__(self, resource_type: str, address: Optional["AccountAddress"] = None):
84        self.resource_type = resource_type
85        self.address = address
86        addr_str = f" on account {address}" if address else ""
87        super().__init__(f"Resource not found: {resource_type}{addr_str}", 404)
resource_type
address
class SerializationError(aptos_sdk.BcsError): 
175class SerializationError(BcsError):
176    """Failed to serialize data to BCS format."""
177
178    def __init__(self, message: str):
179        super().__init__(f"BCS serialization error: {message}")

Failed to serialize data to BCS format.

SerializationError(message: str) 
178    def __init__(self, message: str):
179        super().__init__(f"BCS serialization error: {message}")
class SimulationError(aptos_sdk.TransactionError): 
142class SimulationError(TransactionError):
143    """Transaction simulation failed."""
144
145    def __init__(self, message: str, vm_status: Optional[str] = None):
146        self.vm_status = vm_status
147        status_str = f" (VM status: {vm_status})" if vm_status else ""
148        super().__init__(f"Simulation failed: {message}{status_str}")

Transaction simulation failed.

SimulationError(message: str, vm_status: Optional[str] = None) 
145    def __init__(self, message: str, vm_status: Optional[str] = None):
146        self.vm_status = vm_status
147        status_str = f" (VM status: {vm_status})" if vm_status else ""
148        super().__init__(f"Simulation failed: {message}{status_str}")
vm_status
class TransactionError(aptos_sdk.AptosError): 
105class TransactionError(AptosError):
106    """Base class for transaction-related errors."""

Base class for transaction-related errors.

class TransactionFailed(aptos_sdk.TransactionError): 
127class TransactionFailed(TransactionError):
128    """
129    Transaction failed during execution.
130
131    Attributes:
132        txn_hash: The transaction hash that failed.
133        vm_status: The VM status/error code from execution.
134    """
135
136    def __init__(self, txn_hash: str, vm_status: str):
137        self.txn_hash = txn_hash
138        self.vm_status = vm_status
139        super().__init__(f"Transaction {txn_hash} failed: {vm_status}")

Transaction failed during execution.

Attributes: txn_hash: The transaction hash that failed. vm_status: The VM status/error code from execution.

TransactionFailed(txn_hash: str, vm_status: str) 
136    def __init__(self, txn_hash: str, vm_status: str):
137        self.txn_hash = txn_hash
138        self.vm_status = vm_status
139        super().__init__(f"Transaction {txn_hash} failed: {vm_status}")
txn_hash
vm_status
class TransactionTimeout(aptos_sdk.TransactionError): 
109class TransactionTimeout(TransactionError):
110    """
111    Transaction did not complete within the timeout period.
112
113    Attributes:
114        txn_hash: The transaction hash that timed out.
115        timeout_seconds: The timeout duration in seconds.
116    """
117
118    def __init__(self, txn_hash: str, timeout_seconds: int):
119        self.txn_hash = txn_hash
120        self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds
121        super().__init__(
122            f"Transaction {txn_hash} timed out after {timeout_seconds} seconds. "
123            "The transaction may still be pending."
124        )

Transaction did not complete within the timeout period.

Attributes: txn_hash: The transaction hash that timed out. timeout_seconds: The timeout duration in seconds.

TransactionTimeout(txn_hash: str, timeout_seconds: int) 
118    def __init__(self, txn_hash: str, timeout_seconds: int):
119        self.txn_hash = txn_hash
120        self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds
121        super().__init__(
122            f"Transaction {txn_hash} timed out after {timeout_seconds} seconds. "
123            "The transaction may still be pending."
124        )
txn_hash
timeout_seconds
